item.title: Critical
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: This is the result of a skill check roll that is 1/20 (or 5%) of the regular chance of success. Generally, a critical success is rewarded with greater results, though in some cases a critical success is required for success at all. For example, if the regular chance for success is 75%, any roll of 4 or under is a critical success (1/20 of 75% is 3.75, rounded up to 4).

item.title: Talent Point Level
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Talent Point Level is equal to the amount of Talent Points a hero has. Some things are determined by a character's Talent Point Level (such as Legacy Items, or mana level).

also referred to as Talent Level or TL.

item.title: Save
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: Rolling a characteristic to resist an effect or spell.

item.title: Difficult
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Your skill is halved when a task is harder than average, which is considered a difficult skill check.

Difficult Actions Fighting an opponent you can’t see, climbing a smooth surface, or remembering obscure scientific minutiae are all Difficult tasks. To determine the skill rating or characteristic rating for a Difficult action, divide the appropriate skill or characteristic roll in half (round up).

item.title: Easy
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Your skill is doubled when a task is easier than normal.

Easy Actions: Some actions are Easy, even for the untrained. Sneaking in the dark when there are other loud noises, climbing a tree with many handholds, or recalling the most fundamental points of an academic discipline are all Easy actions. For an Easy action, double the skill rating for the active character. Easy characteristic rolls use a multiplier of ×10 or double the normal characteristic roll. However, success at an Easy skill roll does not merit an experience check.

item.title: Experience Bonus
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: 1/2 INT, added to rolls when leveling up.

item.title: Talents
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: Extraordinary abilities that define your character.

item.title: Characteristic
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: A hero's base physical and mental abilities.

item.title: Special
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: A special success if the roll is equal to or less than 1/5 the skill rating (20%).

item.title: Fumble
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: A roll of a 100 is a fumble. Fumbles never yield any beneficial results, and always end up impeding or even harming your character and/or allies through disastrous or unintended results. A roll of 100 is always a fumble, no matter what the skill rating is.

item.title: Saga Point
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: Saga Points can be used to adjust any dice roll that affects your character. You can use them to fix that natural one you rolled or adjust a low-damage roll. It can also be used to adjust a foe's roll as well. The player spending the Saga Point can determine which of the two dice rolls is the one that counts. A Saga Point can only be used to change a roll once. Saga Points can carry over from session to session. A player who starts the game with zero Saga Points gains at least 1 Saga Point. Saga Points can be used for leveling at the end of a session.

item.title: SIZ
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Size defines height, weight, and bulk. There is no SIZ-associated characteristic roll; on the character sheet the space is used to note your character’s damage modifier. A character’s SIZ, as body mass, helps determine hit points and damage modifier.

Size Chart

item.title: Kai Warrior
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
The kai warrior is one with his inner energy and the world around them. They can take this universal energy, called kai, to protect and defend themselves.

Kai Mastery (2): Your control and discipline has imbued your spirit. Your Spirit (Kai) Point pool is now equal to double your Acumen.

Kai Fist (1, prerequisite Kai Mastery): You imbue your energy into your martial arts. Your unarmed strikes gain lethal 1d6+1 damage and gain the Bludgeoning special.

Kai Protection (1, prerequisite Kai Mastery): You use 2 kai to form a protective force that flows over your skin. It gives you 1 AV, but you must not be impeded by any other armor. You can spend 2 more Kai to gain 1 AV up to AV 4. This effect lasts 8 hours.

Kai Dodge (1, prerequisite Kai Mastery): Dodging melee attacks is not difficult. 

Kai Speed (1, prerequisite Kai Fist): Once per round, you may spend 1 kai to attack another time.

Focused Strike (1, prerequisite Kai Speed): Your unarmed strikes can cause Impaling.

Iron Fist (1, prerequisite Focused Strike): Your unarmed strikes become 2d6+2.

Pure Body (1, prerequisite Kai Protection): You may spend 1 kai to eliminate poison in your body. This takes a standard action.

Unsoiled Body (1, prerequisite Pure Body): You may spend 2 kai to cleanse yourself of any natural or magical disease. This takes 1 hour of meditation.

Floating Step (1, prerequisite Kai Speed): As a free action, you may spend 1 kai to move double your normal speed for a round. Your feet do not touch the ground when you do this, but you must have solid or liquid matter beneath you. 

Fluttering Jump (1, prerequisite Floating Step): You may spend 1 kai to triple your jump.

The Lethal Hand (1, prerequisite Focused Strike): You may spend 3 kai to add an additional 3d6+3 damage to a successful unarmed strike.

item.title: Rituals, Rites, and Reverberations
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
SagaBorn is a low-magic system, but I wanted to give heroes ways to cast some higher-level, more powerful spells. Due to my love for old pulp fantasy magic, where it is dangerous and unknowable, I was led to design a system for rituals. This allows for a mage, or group of mages, to cast powerful spells, but with a potentially hefty cost.

Rituals, Rites, and Reverberations

Rituals are magic rites that allow magic users to cast seemingly impossible spells.

A ritual or rite is performed using Spellcraft checks (normally 3 to 5, but it can be any odd number above 3). For the ritual to be successful, the spellcaster needs more successes than failures. Each failure creates a reverberation, a ripple in the fabric of the current plane. These can have many different effects, each detailed in the Reverberation section.

There are other costs to rituals. Rituals often have components. While some may be very rare and need a quest or adventure to find, most just have a gold value. A mage can buy a batch of “spell components” and just subtract the cost whenever a certain component is needed. Most rituals also have a Horror cost as the mages are bending energy and space into unnatural ways, and this leaves a mark.

And finally, rituals also cost mana. Some rituals cost more mana than a single mage may have. This means the ritual needs more spellcasters, and all contributing spellcasters must participate in all Spellcraft checks. This is done as a Cooperative skill check, with one mage being the lead.

Casting Rituals and Rites

  • Always a spell caster who is the magical focus.
  • A ritual can have as many spellcasters as wanted, but anyone contributing must have some mana.
  • All participating mages will roll Spellcasting skill checks; this will be handled as a cooperative skill check.
  • Additional mages can also contribute to mana. 

Reverberations

When a ritual is being cast, it can cause other magical energy to coalesce and create reverberations in the prime plane. 

Demon

A failed Spellcraft leads to a demon breaking through the veil into the prime plane. The StoryGuide can choose a CV, but it is recommended that it be aligned with the ritual’s difficulty.

Spirit

Undead rise from the mists into the prime plane. The StoryGuide can choose a CV, but it is recommended that it be aligned with the ritual’s difficulty.

Destruction

A ball of dark energy encircles the mages and explodes, doing 2d6 energy damage in a 30’ radius

Sacrifice

The mages feel a clawing in their minds. One must give up mana equal to 2d4+1. This mana lowers their maximum mana for days equal to their Talent Point level.

Horror

All mages and all observers gain 3d6 Horror.

Wild Magic

Magic can be unwieldy, and the ritual has caused an outburst of magic power. One of the mages participating casts a spell. The mage is determined at random, and they roll 2d4 to see how much mana they spend on the spell. While usually the random spell is centered on the focus of the ritual, the SG can decide otherwise. No matter, the burst of wild energy is always disruptive.

Nothing

No noticeable effect affects those at the ritual, but for the next week, they all suffer minor bouts of bad luck.

Example Rituals:

Become Between

Casting Time: 10 minutes

Duration: 1 hour

Materials: Consumed Components (100 gp) - a quartz crystal, a thin piece of fabric, and a silver loop

Skill Checks: 3

Cost: 10 mana

Failure: casters suffer hallucinations for 8 hours

Horror Cost: 2/2d4

A ritual to make someone ethereal, their body hovering between the living world and the In-Between. 

Commune with the Dead

Casting Time: 13 minutes

Duration: 5 minutes

Materials: Consumed Components (10 gp) - Incense, dirt from a grave

Skill Checks: 3 Spellcraft checks

Cost: 5 mana

Failure: Caster is considered Shaken for 1d4 hours.

Horror Cost: 5/2d6

The caster raises the dead to answer 6 questions. The corpse must still have a mouth and can not be undead (though a slain undead that had a 7 or above INT scores can be communed with). 

The corpse only has the knowledge it had in its life (or undeath). The corpse has the same personality it had during its life, but will be more aloof or angry. They are not bound to tell the truth, and answers are often brief and cryptic.

Creating an Automaton

Worker Automaton

Casting Time: 8 hours

Duration: N/A

Materials: Consumed Components (400 gp) - copper and brass, a crystal or glass sphere

Skill Checks: 5 Spellcraft checks

Cost: 30 mana

Failure: All spellcraft is difficult for 2d4 days

Horror: 2/1d6

This creates a worker automaton, a construct that follows the orders of its creator. It can take simple orders and will repeat the last order given by its creator until it is destroyed.

Guardian Automaton

Casting Time: 13 hours

Duration: N/A

Materials: Consumed Components (1000 gp) amarnium and brass, crystal or glass sphere

Skill Checks: 13 Spellcraft checks

Cost: 50 mana

Failure: All spellcraft is difficult for 2d4 days, and mages are fatigued for 48 hours.

Horror: 2/2d4

This creates an automaton, a construct that follows its creator's orders. It can take simple orders and will repeat the last order until it is destroyed.

Creating a Golem

Casting Time: 13 hours

Duration: N/A

Materials: materials based on golem type and a crystal or glass sphere

Skill Checks: 7 Spellcraft checks

Cost: mana based on golem type

Failure: mages are stunned for 24 hours and all spellcraft is difficult for 2d4 days

Horror: 2/1d6

Flesh Golem, Lesser - Consumed Components (400 gp) - a corpse, herbs (must include mandrake root), filtered sand, amber, and blessed clay from a graveyard.

Flesh Golem, Greater - Consumed Components (1300 gp) - a corpses, herbs (must include mandrake root), filtered sand, amber, and blessed clay from a graveyard.

Clay Golem - Consumed Components (1800 gp) - herbs (must include mandrake root), filtered sand, amber, and blessed clay from a graveyard.

Stone Golem, Lesser - Consumed Components (2400 gp) -

Stone Golen Greater - Consumed Components (3200 gp) -

Iron Golem - Consumed Components (6600 gp) -

Exorcism

Casting Time: 1 hour

Duration: Varies

Materials: Consumed Components (30 gp) - silver dust,  water from a spring, salt, ink, and a quill.

Skill Check: 3

Cost: 8 Mana

Failure: -2 to Spirit saves for 1d10 days

Horror Cost: 1/1d3+1

The possessed creature must be trapped within a circle of salt. Runes of protection, expulsion, and binding must be written on the skin of the possessed (Spellcraft Check). Then the silver powder is mixed with the water and forced down the throat of the possessed creature. If the runes are scribed correctly, the invading spirit will be expelled, leaving the body unharmed.

Geas

Casting Time: 20 minutes

Duration: Permanent (Dispel)

Materials: Consumed Components (27 gp) -  ball of wax, a gold coin, and a tourmaline

Skill Checks: 3

Cost: 21 Mana

Failure: -10 to spirit saves for 10 days

Horror Cost: 1/1d6

A powerful enchantment, geas places a binding magical command on a creature, compelling it to either perform a specific task or avoid a particular action, as defined by the caster. While the spell cannot force a creature to commit suicide or undertake an action that would certainly lead to its death, it can demand nearly any other course of behavior. The affected creature is bound to the command until the task is completed, regardless of how long it takes.

The target may attempt to resist the geas with a Spirit check against the caster’s Spellcraft skill. If the creature successfully resists, the caster is immediately aware of the failure.

Should the creature willingly defy the command or fail to actively work toward fulfilling it, it suffers 2d8 damage and loses 1 point of CON for each day of neglect or each deliberate act of disobedience. This damage cannot be avoided with a save.

Hide Aura

Casting Time: 10 minutes

Duration: 1 Day

Materials: Black Wax Candle, Cold Iron Bar (consumed, see text), Silk sheet, Stone

Skill Check: Varies

Mana Cost: Varies

Failure: Loss of components

Horror Cost: 0

A magic item can be hidden from detection spells with the use of this ritual. 

A sheet of silk is laid on the ground and then the stone is laid upon it. The magical item is placed on top and then the cold iron bar is placed over it. The silk is used to wrap the stone and the iron close to the magical item. The candle is burned and the wax is used to seal the silk. This stops the magic item from being detected by scrying or detect spells. This protection lasts for 24 hours. The cold iron ingot is slowly consumed and turned to slag as the spell works, and after 24 hours it is left useless.

Charged Magic Items (per charge):

Skill Check: 1

Mana Cost: 3

Cold Iron Worth: 1 gp

Permanent Magic Item:

Skill Check: 3

Mana Cost: 7

Cold Iron Worth: 3 gp

Artifact:

Skill Check: 5 Difficult

Mana Cost: 20

Cold Iron Worth: 20 gp

Portent

Casting Time: 1 hour

Duration: Varies

Materials: Silver pendulum (100 gp); Consumed Components (20 gp) - Water from a spring. Salt, Pepper, A quartz crystal (1 gp, will crumble to dust)

Skill Checks: 3 

Cost: 1/2 Max HP for 2 days

Failure: 1/4 Max HP for 3d6 days

Horror Cost: 0/1d3

A portent can present players with a visual riddle that provides answers, but sometimes reveals questions they didn’t know they had.

Portent, Greater 

Casting Time: 8 hour

Duration: Varies

Materials: Silver pendulum (100 gp); Consumed Components (500 gp) - Water from a spring. Salt, Pepper, A diamond (will crumble to dust)

Skill Checks: 9

Cost: 1/2 Max HP for 3 days for each mage

Failure: 1/4 Max HP for 4d6 days

Horror Cost: 4/1d8

A portent can give the players a visual riddle that will give them answers, but sometimes to questions they didn’t know they had. The greater portent can answer more than one question and the mages can direct the riddle to try and reveal more clues. 

When a Greater Portent is being cast, the wall between this world and the navirim becomes thin. Creatures can enter this world during this time. Weather patterns also shift and change around a Greater Portent.

Removal of a Curse

Casting Time: 1 hours

Duration: Instant

Materials: Silver Bowl (10 gp); Consumed Components (100 gp) - squid’s ink, tallow, blood of the cursed, blood of an uncursed, charcoal

Skill Checks: 5

Cost: 7 mana

Failure: curse becomes more powerful

Horror Cost: 2/2d4

A candle must be made of tallow from a virgin boar mixed with the blood of the cursed and the oak charcoal. The cursed must have runic inscriptions written on their body with the squid’s ink. On a night were the moons are waning, the candle is placed in a bowl of water. The bowl must be made of silver and engraved with the name of the cursed.

The mage and the cursed must begin to chant “We pull from the bearer this unwanted curse, by this flame may its power diminish and disperse”. The mage must focus and attempt to pull the energy of the curse into the flame by using mana and concentration.

When the flame reaches the water level, but before it is extinguished, the blood of an uncursed must be spilt into the bowl. It must be fresh blood. As the flame is quenched in the water, the curse will be lifted. Best when Moons are waning, and in a place of power.

Summoning a Creature from the In-Between

This spell will summon a creature stuck in the Between to the material plane of Uteria. This does not contain the summoned creature, it only frees them.

Casting Time: 8 hours

Materials: Consumed Components (100 gp) - A vessel for the creature, 5 red and 5 white candles imbued with iron and silver, juniper incense, summoning circle outlined in silver

Skill Checks: 7

Cost: 23 mana

Failure: Loss of 1d8 hit points for 5 days

Sanity Cost: 2/1d6

The circle is drawn in a place in view of the night sky and moons, and the vessel body is placed in the center of the circle. The candles are set at equal distance apart. The mages all begin to chant and write the name of the summoned along the outside of the circle, over and over. This is repeated for 8 hours. If successful, the summoned creature invades the body of the vessel. 

This does not bring over a summoned creature's body if it is in the In-Between. Creatures can be sent back using the same ritual.

*Spellcasters gain +5% to their Spellcraft rolls for each full moon.

Summon Greater Navirite

Casting Time: 13 hours

Duration: 1 hour

Materials: Consumed Components (3333 gp) - A single candle whose wax is mixed with the blood of the caster, silver wire, 5 gold runestones, amarnium hooks

Skill Checks: 13

Cost: 66 mana (mana is lost from max mana and regained at 1 mana per day)

Failure: shaken for 3d10 days, gain 1 phobia

Sanity Cost: 5/5d4

The circle is drawn in a place untouched by sun or moon. The single candle is lit on the northern side. A net is woven of the silver wire, with the amarnium hooks attached. The five runestones are set equidistant apart along the circle.

The casters begin the ritual, weaving the magic to enchant the net to hold the greater being. As they weave, the begin to tear the rift to the Navirim.

If the ritual is successfully cast, the creature is bound in the net. They will remain prisoner for one hour, and will be subject to answer questions or be bound by another ritual to serve the mages.

item.title: Elemental Ability
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. 
  • Water puts out mundane fires.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Athletics savings throw to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, .or full plate).
  • Air knocks Medium or smaller creatures prone. Reflex savings throw to negate falling.

item.title: Magic Damage
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: Damage done by magic spells. This often bypasses AV, unless the armor is made of Cold Iron or has other magic negating properties.

item.title: Elemental Damage
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: This damage is caused by both mundane and magic elements, such as wind, fire, water, or electricity. Elemental damage typically bypasses AV, unless the armor has element negating properties.

item.title: Damage Modifier
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: A modifier to rolled damage due to above-average Strength and Size characteristics.

item.title: D100
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
A d100 is a 100-sided die. In SagaBorn it is used to determine success or fail for skill checks.

There are two common ways to roll a d100: 2 d10's rolled as percentiles, or an actual d100 die. but there is a better way!

The Best Option: Perfect Percentile – A Twenty-Sided d100

Roll with precision and style. The Perfect Percentile brings together the classic charm of the original retro d20 with the smooth functionality of a d100 system. Designed and made by SagaBorn.

🔥 Features:

  • Dual icosahedrons marked 0–09 and 00–90, for true d100 rolls.
  • Designed to give the great rolling randomness of a d20 to a d100.
Buy Now $10
Buy Now $10

Old Common Options:

Option 1: Two d10s as Percentiles
One d10 represents the tens digit (00, 10, 20… up to 90).

  • The other d10 represents the ones digit (0–9).
  • Together, they give a number from 01 to 100.
  • Rolling 00 and 0 = 100
  • Rolling 30 and 7 = 37

Option 2: Actual d100 Die

A rare, physical 100-sided die (called a Zocchihedron).

  • It looks like a ball and can take a long time to stop rolling.
  • Cool, but not practical for most players.

item.title: Dark Return
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
The core setting for SagaBorn.

It is a gothic fantasy TTRPG setting set in the lands of Atheles. You can visit https://www.darkreturn.com/ for a full encyclopedia of the world of the Dark Return.

item.title: SagaBorn
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
SagaBorn is the name of the TTRPG system created by Michael Bielaczyc. It is also what the heroes of the wo0rld of the Dark Return are called.

The elves have a tale about the Saaga Ute, translated to common as SagaBorn, a mythical hero who is born to save the world. They believe that Dalimar Gorfiriand was the last SagaBorn the world has seen. The elves have a long history fraught with wars, heroes, and villains. With their core view of themselves in the universe, if one is to believe their assertion that there are many inhabited planets throughout the stars, a Saag Ute must be indeed powerful. 

This is not my humble view. I see the people, races, and creatures of the world as all having a hand in the future of our existence, and therefore many could be SagaBorn. The SagaBorn are those who do not stand by and watch the river of time pass by. They are the ones who make the river change course, slow down, or speed up. They do things large and small that add up to change our world and our being. 

Our world is changing with or without us, and I see many who have risen up to try and make the change for the better. More than just a single hero, but that does not diminish the tales and legends that one can create. This is what we need, what the world needs. We need more to rise up and become SagaBorn.

- Aledyn
Druid 

Visit https://www.sagaborn.com to see our full line of games.

item.title: Ability
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: Something a character can do or feel, whether a skill, a passion, or some other factor rated on a 1–100 rating. Generally, when you roll an ability successfully, you get an experience check.

item.title: Armor Value
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: The numeric value representing how much damage armor absorbs or mitigates each time a character is hit. Shorthand for Armor Value is AV.

item.title: Base Range
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: The normal distance within which a missile weapon can hit a target.

item.title: Combat Round
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: A 10-second amount of time imposed during combat or dramatic activities, where the order that actions occur is important. This is game time, not actual time—the events of a few seconds of action may take several minutes to play out between the players and StoryGuide.

item.title: StoryGuide
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: The director, or guide for the game, who helps you create characters, comes up with the adventure, and represents the world outside your character, rolling dice for the nonplayer characters and the opposing forces.

item.title: Hit Points
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: A measure of the relative health of your character, represented in a value derived from their Constitution and Size characteristics. Attackers inflict damage in hit points, subtracted from the target’s total hit points. Usually when your character reaches 0 hit points, they fall unconscious and when they reach -10 hit points, they are dead.

item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: Roll 1d10 and add DEX at the start off a combat round. This sorts people into the order in which they can act in turn-based combat.

item.title: Acid Bolt
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Dodge, Parry*

Special: Bleeding

Type: Damage

A bolt of acid flies from the mage’s outstretched hand toward the target. Acid bolt does 1d4+1 damage per mana spent plus acts as Acid. A mage must successfully use Aim to hit the target.

Spellcraft failure: the bolt misses the target.

item.title: Acid Fog
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Acid Fog

Mana per Level: 3

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: AoE 5’ Radius Sphere per mana level

Duration: 1d4 rounds per mana level

Avoidance: None

Special: Double Duration, Critical: Max Damage and Duration

Type: Damage

A dense fog of corrosive acid coalesces in the area. The acid does 1d4 damage to creatures and other exposed objects. The acid also reduces Armor Value of any armor worn by creatures in this area by 1 per round. The acid does initial damage as the spell is cast, and then does damage to creatures still in the fog at the end of their turn. Inanimate objects not carried or wielded by creatures take damage at the end of the turn. 

Targeting: A caster must target a single square and then successfully roll a Spot skill check to hit that target. If the Spot skill fails, roll 1d8, and the spell lands in an adjoining space. To determine the direction, the closest space to the spellcaster being 1 and rotating clockwise. To determine how far from the intended target space, it is equal to 5’x the spells damage dice away from the target space. On a fumble (100), the spell centers on the caster. For more see Targeting AoE Spells previously in this chapter.

The only way to avoid the acid fog is to leave the affected area. Water or bases can dilute the acid, causing half damage. Once out of the fog, the acid dissipates from any person or object.

Spellcraft failure: the cloud fails to materialize.

Spellcraft fumble: the cloud centers on caster.

item.title: Aid
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 minute

Avoidance: None

Special: Doubles Bonus

Type: Buff

The target gains +10 per mana spent on a chosen skill until that skill is used.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and the mage loses the mana spent.

item.title: Animal Friend
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Animal Friend

Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 minute per mana

Avoidance: Spirit

Special:

Type: Utility

You may use Persuasion with animals. Each level of mana allows you +10% to your Persuasion skill when dealing with animals. The communication between the mage and the animal is simple, with emotions or simple directions from the caster being understood by the animal. The caster can also gain simple understanding of the animal's wants or needs.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Babble
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Concentration

Avoidance: Spirit

Special: Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult

Type: Mind

Babble causes the target only to be able to make incoherent noises and sounds. To themselves, they sound as if they are speaking normally, but others can not understand the strange noises they are making. The more mana imbued in the spell, the more serious the babbling becomes. The spell lasts as long as the spellcaster concentrates on it, though the target gets to attempt to save each round on their turn. Any mana level of babble causes all communication and spellcasting by the target to become Difficult.

1 Mana: The target's words become incoherent.

2 Mana: The target can not stop speaking, feeling compelled to talk about anything while no one understands them.

3 Mana: The target is frantically babbling, not even making sense to themselves, and all actions are considered Difficult.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Bear’s Strength
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 2

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 minute

Avoidance: None

Special: None

Type: Buff

The target becomes stronger, gaining magical strength. 

2 Mana: +10 to STR

4 Mana: +20 to STR

6 Mana: +30 to STR

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Befriend
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 3

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 30’

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 Hour per ACU

Avoidance: Charm

Special: Double Duration

Type: Mind

The target sees the spellcaster as a friend. The target will engage with them in a friendly manner, but will not do anything that would harm themselves or others they consider allies. Attempts to Persuade them become Easy, unless it is dangerous or against their beliefs. They are still fully capable of their own actions and thoughts and lose no memories of past interactions with the spellcaster or their allies.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and the mana is lost.

item.title: Bend Sound
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Varies

Target: Varies

Duration: 1 round per level

Avoidance: Agility for half unless otherwise stated

Special: Double damage. Critical: Double damage maxxed.

Type: Damage

Mana 1: You produce a loud, piercing sound that can break fragile, non-magical items, temporarily deafen an opponent, or harm creatures made of crystal.

When used in an area, this effect shatters non-magical objects made of crystal, glass, ceramic, or porcelain within a 5-foot radius. These objects are broken into pieces unless they weigh more than one pound per your caster level. All exposed fragile or crystalline objects and crystalline creatures also take 1d6 sonic damage.

You can also deafen one target for 2d4 rounds with a successful attack. The target can attempt a Fortitude save to halve the duration.

Mana 3: You can manipulate sound to counter and dispel a Silence spell by making a Heroic action against the opposing caster's Spellcraft skill.

Mana 5: You unleash a powerful blast of sound across a 30’ area, causing each creature in it to suffer 3d8 sonic damage. A successful Stamina save is required to avoid being stunned for 1 round. Creatures that are deaf cannot be stunned but still take damage. All exposed fragile or crystalline objects and crystalline creatures also take 1d6 sonic damage.This effect does not penetrate a Silence spell; to bypass it, the Silence spell must be dispelled first.

Mana 7: You release a devastating, high-pitched scream that deafens and injures all creatures in its 60’ cone range. Affected creatures are deafened for 2d6 rounds and take 5d6 sonic damage, with a Spirit save to resist deafness and Agility to halve the damage. All exposed fragile or crystalline objects and crystalline creatures also take 1d6 sonic damage. This effect also cannot penetrate a Silence spell and requires dispelling the silence to take effect fully.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and the mana is lost.

item.title: Blur
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 minute

Avoidance: None

Special: Double Duration

Type: Buff

4 mana: The spell’s target shifts in this plane, making them harder to see and touch. Any attack against the target becomes difficult.

7 mana: The spell’s target becomes difficult to see, making attacks against them difficult, as well as allowing the blurred creature to move through most solid objects. Iron or steel block blurred creatures.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and mana is lost.

item.title: Cat’s Dexterity
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 2

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 minute

Avoidance: None

Special:

Type: Buff

The target becomes more agile, gaining magical speed. 

2 Mana: +10 to DEX

4 Mana: +20 to DEX

6 Mana: +30 to DEX

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and mana is lost.

item.title: Command
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Spirit / Contested

Special:  Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult

Type: Mind

You issue a command to a creature, compelling them to obey, whether it's simple or complex. The complexity of the command affects the mana cost, as does the duration required for the creature to complete the task. The spell has no effect on undead or creatures that do not understand your language. It cannot force a creature to kill itself or engage in actions that would guarantee its death, but it can command nearly any other course of action.

  • 1 Mana: A single-word command, such as "drop," "halt," or "flee." The target follows the command on its next action and can do nothing else for one round.
  • 3 Mana: A simple command like "pick up that sword" or "don’t harm him." The target follows the command on its next action and does nothing else for one round per caster level.
  • 5 Mana: A more detailed command, such as "throw all your weapons into the river" or "stop fighting and behave." The target obeys on its next action and continues for 1 minute per mana spent.
  • 7 Mana: A complex directive, requiring the target to perform a service or refrain from an action as specified. The target begins obeying on its next action and must continue for one day per caster level or until the task is completed.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and mana is lost.

Spell avoided: Target becomes dazed for rounds equal to mana spent.

item.title: Conjure Creature
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Empty Space

Duration: 2 rounds per mana

Avoidance: None

Special: Creature gains +2 damage

Type: Summoning

This spell draws the consciousness of a creature from the Navirim and places it within a body made of the caster’s mana. The summoned creature appears at the chosen location and acts immediately, taking its turn along with the caster. It fights for you with its full abilities.

By using more mana, the caster can summon stronger creatures.

Examples of Summoned Creatures:

The summoned beings are not true living creatures but projections of the caster's power, each with similar abilities to real creatures. They may appear in different forms, reflecting the caster's personality or desires, and can take on the form of elemental creatures if the caster chooses.

Common Creature Options: Imp (Demon), Wolf, Fox, Large Cat

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and mana is lost.

item.title: Countermagic
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: Reaction

Range: 100’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Spellcraft / Contested

Special: None

Type: Debuff

You may cast Countermagic as a reaction to interrupt another caster’s spell. State your intention to cast Countermagic and the amount of mana you’ll use before learning the opponent’s spell mana cost. Both casters roll a Heroic Action using their Spellcraft skill, with the higher successful roll prevailing. Countermagic disrupts the magical energy of the targets spell according to the mana spent on Countermagic. If the mana spent on counter magic meets or exceeds the amount spent on the original casting, then the targeted spell is canceled. If it is less, this will lower the power of the targeted spell equal to the Countermagic spent mana.

For example, Mingus casts Elemental Blast. Tersh reacts with Countermagic. Tersh declares the are spending 4 mana and they roll a 56 Spellcraft. Mingus rolls their Spellcraft to contest the Countermagic, but they only roll a 34. Tersh wins the contest, but Mingus had spent 6 mana on their Elemental Blast. Tersh’s Countermagic cancels 4 mana worth of the Elemental Blast, causing it to only be cast at a 2 mana level, though Mingus still spends 6 mana.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

Spell avoided: The spell fails, and the opposing caster still expends their mana.

item.title: Creeping Element
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: N/A

Special: Double Damage

Type: Damage

A chosen element forms around the target, seeping into their skin doing elemental damage.

Each mana level does 1d4+1 damage plus its elemental bonus. Creeping element ignores the AV of the target creature.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires or dilutes acid. Water can also choke the target, causing it to become Panicked. A successful Spirit check to resists this fear.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Effort save to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures and causing blindness for 1 round. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air chokes the target, causing it to become Panicked. A successful Spirit check to resists this fear.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Cure Disease
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 hour

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: N/A

Special: Double the bonus

Type: Healing

Cure disease gives the target a +10% to their Stamina the next time they save versus a disease. For each mana level spent, the spell adds an additional +10% to the save.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Horror
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: An optional system where your character’s mental health is measured by their capacity to withstand horrific sights, events, and revelations. As Horror increases, the grasp on reality diminishes. If your character takes too much Horror damage at once, they may experience detrimental effects.

item.title: Opposed Roll
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
When a skill check is in opposition of another PC or NPC skill check, this is an opposed roll.

Sometimes a character tries to do something, and that action is contested by another. This will lead to a contested roll. Contested rolls are Skill vs Skill, or Characteristic vs Characteristic. When rolling a contested roll the two parties roll and compare results. The highest successful roll is the winner, unless a special or critical roll is rolled. A special beats a high success roll, and a critical beats a special. 

For example, Worric tries to push past an ogre in a tight hallway. The Ogre rolls a 78, and its Effort is 80, meaning it has a very high success roll, and looks to be the clear winner. Worric rolls a 12, and his Effort is 65. Worric’s success is special, so even though it is lower, it wins.

item.title: Cooperative Roll
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
When two or more characters work together on a skill.

Two or more characters can attempt to work together on an action, if reasonable. This is a type of augment. Your StoryGuide may impose restrictions on how many characters can contribute to a particular activity, as it may be limited by physical or other practical concerns. Some tasks simply cannot be assisted, depending on circumstances.

Whenever working together on an action, pick one character as the lead. All the other characters pitching in should make skill rolls. Take the best result or a fumble, if one occurs.

• Critical Success: Modify the primary character’s skill rating by +50%.

• Special Success: Modify the skill rating by +20%.

• Success: Modify the skill rating by +10%.

• Failure: If no helpers succeeded, the primary character’s skill rating is modified by –10%.

• Fumble: A fumble from any helper is so distracting that the primary skill is modified by –50%.

One reason for cooperative skill use is to increase the chance of the primary character’s success, as well as the chance of special and critical successes. If the primary skill roll is successful, everyone involved who made a successful skill roll can make an experience check. If not, no one gets an experience check. Another reason for cooperative skill use is when direct assistance is not possible, such as helping provide guidance from afar. Some skills may not be able to be done cooperatively.

item.title: Aim
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Aim allows you to bypass 1/2 AV with a Successful Spot.

Once per round, you may attempt to aim an attack that will hit a specific part of target. An aimed attack is Difficult by default. With a successful Heroic Action (Spot, Sense) you can aim, and your attack is at your standard skill rating.  

An aimed shot can do one of two things: It may negate 1/2 AV of worn armor*. Or it can target a specific part of the body. This can have many effects, and it is up to the player and SG to determine the effect.

*Some creatures have natural AV, so bypassing it would only happen if they have known weak spots. AV is rounded up.

item.title: Reaction
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: You can use a Free Action to react to an event. Some abilities, talents, and spells can be used as a reaction, but not all skills or talents.

item.title: Dark
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 3

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Dodge

Special:

Type: Utility

This spell causes an object to radiate shadowy illumination out to a 20’ radius (every level expands it by another 20’). All creatures in the area become concealed, making any actions towards them Difficult . Even creatures that can normally see in such conditions (such as with dark vision) have the miss chance in an area shrouded in magical darkness.

Normal lights (torches, candles, lanterns, and so forth) are incapable of brightening the area, as are light spells of lower level.

If Dark is cast on a small object that is then placed inside or under a light-proof covering, the spell’s effect is blocked until the covering is removed.

Darkness counters and dispels a Light spell (one with the light descriptor) by a Heroic action vs the opposing mage using the Spellcraft Skill.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Dispel
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level:1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 100’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: None

Special: None

Type: Utility

A mage can use dispel to rid themselves or another target of the effects of a spell. Unlike Countermagic, it must overcome the mana of the spell targeted, but unlike Countermagic, there is no Heroic Action to negate the spell. Dispel must be targeted at a single spell, and can not effect multiple spells even if they effect the same target. The casting mage must be aware of the spell to target it. Dispel may also be used against summoned creatures or magical traps.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Elemental Arrow
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Varies

Target: Single or MultipleTarget

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Dodge

Special: Impaling

Type: Damage

A magical arrow of magical force or a chosen energy type (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) springs from your hand and speeds to its target. A mage must successfully use Aim to hit the target. You cast a separate arrow for each mana spent, and you may direct each arrow at a different target. Arrows must all be of a matching elemental energy. 

1 Mana: 1d8+3 Damage 60 ft. Range.

Each additional level adds another arrow, allowing for multiple targets (one per arrow), and 60’ to the range.

If the spell is imbued with an element, the spell also causes the Elemental Effect to any target that it does damage to.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires or dilute acid.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Effort save to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air knocks SIZ 20 or smaller creatures prone. Agility save to negate falling.

Spellcraft failure: Your arrow misses its target. For a fumble, use the Missle Fumble Chart.

item.title: Elemental Blast
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 2

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Varies

Target: AoE 5’ Radius Sphere per mana level

Duration: 1 minute per mana

Avoidance: Dodge for half

Special: Dazes

Type: Damage

A small ball of energy emits from your hand traveling to an intended target area and bursts into a spherical blast of elemental energy. You can choose any elemental energy (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) available to you that you have control over, or it can be a generic magical blast. 

2 Mana: 1d6+1 damage, 5 ft. radius sphere, 30 ft. range. 

Each additional level adds 1d6+1 damage, 5’ to the radius sphere, and 30’ to the range.

Targeting: A caster must target a single square and then successfully roll a Spot skill check to hit that target. If the Spot skill fails, roll 1d8, and the spell lands in an adjoining space. To determine the direction, the closest space to the spellcaster being 1 and rotating clockwise. To determine how far from the intended target space, it is equal to 5’x the spells damage dice away from the target space. On a fumble (100), the spell centers on the caster. For more see Targeting AoE Spells previously in this chapter.

If the spell is imbued with an element, the spell also causes the Elemental Effect to any target that it does damage to.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires or dilute acid.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Effort save to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air knocks SIZ 20 or smaller creatures prone. Agility save to negate falling.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles

item.title: Elemental Shield
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 3

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Self

Target: Self

Duration: 3 rounds per mana level

Avoidance: None

Special: see below

Type: Damage

This spell wreaths you in magical or elemental energy (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) and causes damage to each creature that attacks you in melee.

Any creature striking you with its body or a handheld weapon deals normal damage, but at the same time the attacker takes 1d6+1 points of damage. Creatures wielding weapons with reach are not subject to this damage if they attack you. The shield gains 1d6+1 damage per mana level.

The special powers of each version are as follows:

Magical Force

An invisible shield surrounds you doing the spells standard damage to any that hit you.

Fire Shield

The flames are warm to the touch. You take only half damage from ice-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. You cast light for 30 ft. Successful attacks from water or ice-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Cold Shield

The shimmering shield is cool to the touch. You take only half damage from fire-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. 

Water Shield

This liquid shield flows over your body. You take only half damage from fire and earth-based attacks, as well as diluting acid attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save.

Electric Shield

Arcs of lightning crisscross your body. You take only half damage from air and earth-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. Successful attacks from water-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Earth Shield

Rough earth covers your body. You take only half damage from fire-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. 

Air Shield

An invisible barrier covers you. You take only half damage from air and electrical-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. Successful attacks from earth-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Spell Failure: You lose any mana spent on conjuring the shield.

item.title: Elemental Storm
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 2

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Varies

Target: AoE Radius Sphere per mana level

Duration: 1d4 rounds

Avoidance: Dodge for half

Special: No save

Type: Damage

A magical force or a chosen element (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) rains down from above in the area of effect. This spells needs space above the ground of at least 30’ (meaning it can not be cast inside rooms or cramped dungeons).

2 Mana: 1d4+1 damage, 10 ft. radius sphere, 50 ft. range. 

Each additional level adds 1d4+1 damage, 5’ to the radius sphere, and 15’ to the range.

Targeting: A caster must target a single square and then successfully roll a Spot skill check to hit that target. If the Spot skill fails, roll 1d8, and the spell lands in an adjoining space. To determine the direction, the closest space to the spellcaster being 1 and rotating clockwise. To determine how far from the intended target space, it is equal to 5’x the spells damage dice away from the target space. On a fumble (100), the spell centers on the caster. For more see Targeting AoE Spells previously in this chapter.

If the spell is imbued with an element, the spell also causes the Elemental Effect to any target that it does damage to.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires or dilute acid.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Effort save to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air knocks SIZ 20 or smaller creatures prone. Agility save to negate falling.

Spellcraft failure: Roll 1d8, and the spell lands in the adjoining space from the target space, with the closest space to the spellcaster being 1 and rotating clockwise. For a fumble, the spell centers on the caster.

Spellcraft Fumble: The spell targets the caster.

item.title: Elemental Sphere
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana: 3

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 100’

Target: 5 ft. diameter sphere

Duration: 2 rounds per mana

Avoidance: Dodge for half

Special: Double damage, Critical: Max damage

Type: Damage

A globe of elemental energy rolls in whichever direction you point, and strikes a target. It moves 30 ft. per round. If it enters a space with a creature, it stops moving for the round and deals an effect based on the elemental type. An Elemental Sphere rolls over barriers less than 4 ft. tall. You can choose any elemental energy (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) available to you that you have control over.

An Elemental Sphere gains the following powers based on the type of element.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires and does 3d6 damage to creatures of the elemental type fire.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2 and does 2d6 cold damage. 
  • Earthen spikes impale the target for 2d6 damage. Caster may try to surround and grapple the target with the Sphere with a successful Heroic action (Intellect vs target Agility). If successful, the target is grappled and the sphere can not move until the target is let loose.
  • Electricity does 2d6 damage, with an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air causes a Medium or smaller target to lift into the air 10’ and crash down for 1d6 damage. The target can be moved up to 10 ft. and lands prone. This increases by 10’ and 1d6 damage per mana.

The Elemental Sphere moves as long as you actively direct it (Concentration); otherwise, it merely stays at rest. The surface of the Sphere has a soft, yielding consistency and so does not cause damage except by its elemental energy. It cannot push aside unwilling creatures or batter down large obstacles. An Elemental Sphere winks out if it exceeds the spell’s range. Each mana level gives the effect +1d6.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Elemental Wall
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 min per mana

Avoidance: None

Special: None

Type: Damage

You conjure a wall of elemental energy to block a path or create a protective ring around yourself. Choose an element you can control (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) for the wall’s energy.

  • Mana 1: A 10 ft. long, 10 ft. tall, and 1 ft. thick wall dealing 1d4+1 damage to anyone passing through it.
  • Mana 3: A 20 ft. long, 10 ft. tall, and 1 ft. thick wall dealing 3d4+3 damage to anyone passing through it.
  • Mana 5: A 40 ft. long, 10 ft. tall, and 1 ft. thick wall dealing 3d6+3 damage to anyone passing through. The wall can encircle the caster with a 5 ft. radius and can be positioned off-center, allowing space for other medium or small creatures.
  • Mana 7: A 50 ft. long, 10 ft. tall, and 1 ft. thick wall dealing 5d6+5 damage to anyone passing through. The wall can encircle the caster with a 10 ft. radius, with room for medium or small creatures if positioned off-center. Additionally, the caster may cause one side of the wall to emit energy, dealing 1d4+1 damage to anyone within 10 ft

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Fear
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Varies

Duration: 1 minute

Avoidance: Spirit

Special: Doubles Duration

Type: Mind

You cause fear to creatures based on the mana spent. 

Mana 1: With a successful Spellcraft, a single target becomes Shaken.

Mana 3: With a successful Spellcraft, a single target becomes Scared.

Mana 5: With a successful Spellcraft, up to three creatures become Scared.

Mana 7: With a successful Spellcraft, up to three creatures become Panicked.

After the initial round, a Scared or Panicked creature may roll a Spirit check Vs the spellcaster’s Spellcraft skill. With a successful save, the target recovers and only becomes Shaken for the duration of the spell.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Find
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana: 5

Casting Time: 1 hour

Range: 15 miles

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: None

Special: None

Type: Utility

This spell helps you locate a person or object familiar to you. As you turn, you sense when you are facing the direction of the target, provided it is within range. If the target is moving, you also know its direction of travel.

The spell can find a specific creature, a known item, or an item of a particular kind. However, it cannot locate creatures of a general type. To locate a kind of creature, you must have seen one up close (within 30 feet) at least once.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Fox’s Fascination
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 2

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 minute

Avoidance: None

Special: Double the duration

Type: Buff

The target becomes more charismatic. 

2 Mana: +10 to SOC

4 Mana: +20 to SOC

6 Mana: +30 to SOC

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Gain Spirit
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 3

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: None

Special:

Type: Buff

The target recovers 1 lost Spirit Point per 3 mana spent.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and doesn’t work.

item.title: Heal
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Dodge

Special: Add 1d4 healing

Type: Healing

When laying your hand upon a living creature, you channel energy that cures the target.

1 Mana: heals 1d6 +3

Each additional mana adds 1d6+3

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Identify
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 20 Minutes

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: None

Special:

Type: Utility

The spell reveals all magical properties of a single item, including activation methods (if applicable) and any remaining charges. When used on an artifact, Identify may not yield complete information but can offer hints about the artifact's abilities.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Illusionary Image
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 300’

Target: Empty space

Duration: Concentration

Avoidance: Intellect

Special:  Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult

Type: Mind

Mana 1: The illusion can be seen, but lacks sound, smell, texture, or temperature.

Mana 3: The illusion can be seen and has minor sounds, but not speech, smell, texture, or temperature.

Mana 5: The illusion can be seen and has full speech, smell, and temperature.

Mana 7: The illusion can be seen and has full speech, smell, texture, and temperature.

This spell creates the visual illusion of an object, creature, or force, as visualized by you. 

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Imbue Staff
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 2 minutes

Range: Touch

Target: Self

Duration: 4 hours

Avoidance: None

Special: Doubles duration

Type: Buff

1 Mana: The spellcaster’s staff gains +5% to hit and +1d4 subdual damage.

3 Mana: The spellcaster’s staff gains +10% to hit and +2d4 subdual damage.

5 Mana: The spellcaster’s staff gains +15% to hit and +3d4 subdual damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Imbue Weapon
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single weapon

Duration: 2 minutes

Avoidance: None

Special: Doubles duration

Type: Buff

1 Mana: The weapon gains +5% to hit and +1 damage.

3 Mana: The weapon gains +10% to hit and +2 damage.

5 Mana: The weapon gains +15% to hit and +3 damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Invulnerable Sphere
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target or Space

Duration: 1 minute

Avoidance: None

Special: Doubles duration and absorption

Type: Buff

The mage creates an invulnerable sphere that absorbs damage, protecting anyone inside. With a single mana worth of energy, the sphere is 5’ in diameter and absorbs 5 hit points worth of damage. For each mana level beyond the first, it can be expanded another 5’ or abosob another 5 damage. This does not block any objects coming out of the protected sphere, nor does it protects against non damaging spells.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Levitate
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 30’ per mana

Target: Single Target

Duration: 2 minutes per mana

Avoidance: Spirit

Special:

Type: Utility

Levitate makes a creature or object levitate. It takes one mana per 10 SIZ to levitate. The target may be raised or lowered 30’ per mana each round. The spellcaster most concentrate on this task. While the spellcaster can not move the target horizontally, the target may pull itself along a surface, such as a wall, to also change their position as they rise or lower.

Attacking while levitating is Difficult. If the spell ends the target floats slowly to the ground.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Light
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Creatures or objects

Duration: Varies

Avoidance: Varies

Special: None, Critical: Max Damage

Type: Utility

Mana 1: Light will cause a single object to glow like a torch, lighting up an area of 30’. Light taken into an area of magical darkness does not function. It lasts 1 hour.

Mana 2: You create up to four lights that resemble lanterns or torches (and cast that amount of light), or up to four glowing spheres of light (which look like will-o’-wisps), or one faintly glowing, vaguely creature-like shape. They must stay within 10 ft. of each other. The lights move at 100 ft. per round. You can use these lights to blind or distract a foe giving them a -5% to all skills until the light spell is canceled or the caster stops concentrating. These lights can also be used to outline hidden foes. Outlined creatures do not benefit from the concealment normally provided by darkness-based effects, Blur, Displacement, Invisibility, or similar effects.

Mana 3: You may enchant an item that sheds light as bright as full daylight in a 60 ft. radius, and dim light for an additional 60 ft. beyond that. Creatures that take penalties in bright light also take them while within the radius of this magical light. This is not the equivalent of daylight for the purposes of creatures that are damaged or destroyed by bright light. A mana 3 (or above) Light spell counters and dispels a Darkness spell (one with the darkness descriptor) by a Heroic action vs the opposing mage using the Spellcraft Skill.

If cast on a small object that is then placed inside or under a light-proof covering, the spell’s effects are blocked until the covering is removed.

Mana 5: Focusing light like a ray of the sun, you project a blast of light from your open palm. A creature struck takes 2d8 points of damage. An undead creature takes an additional 2d8 damage.

Mana 7: A 10 ft. glowing ball of light energy comes into existence casting light as bright as the sun. You may summon this within the 100 ft. range of the spell, and the light fills a 300 ft. radius. Any within 40 ft. of the sphere take 3d8 damage. Undead take 6d8 damage. This is the equivalent of daylight for the purposes of creatures that are damaged or destroyed by bright light.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Mage Bolt
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target per bolt

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: None

Special: Doubles Damage. Critical: Double Damage Maxed.

Type: Damage

A bolt of magical energy darts forth from your fingertip and strikes its target, dealing 1d4+1 points of force damage. The bolt cannot miss if the spellcaster can see the target. Specific areas of the target can not be singled out.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Mage Compass
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: None

Special: Adds +2 to the bonus. Critical: Doubles the bonus.

Type: Utility

Mage compass bestows directional sense to it’s target. This gives a 5% bonus to the spell target in Navigation skill checks per mana level. 

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Phantasmal Hands
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: concentration

Avoidance: Dodge

Special:

Type: Utility/Damage

A spectral version of your hands appear to lift or damage objects or creatures.

1 mana: You point your finger at a SIZ 1 object (up to 20 lbs) and can lift it and move it at will from a distance. As a Move Action, you can propel the object as far as 15 ft. in any direction, though the spell ends if the distance between you and the object ever exceeds the spell’s range.

2 Mana: Same as above, but now you can lift or move up to SIZ 6 (60 lbs).

3 Mana: Same as above, but now you can lift or move up to SIZ 13 (110 lbs).

5 Mana: Same as above, but now you can lift or move up to SIZ 19 (225 lbs). If desired, you can use the force of your Phantasmal Hands to also pummel or pull a target for 2d6 damage per round as well as move it. 

7 Mana: Same as above, but now you can lift or move up to SIZ 25 (550 lbs), and cause 4d6+4 damage per round. 

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Phase
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Spirit

Special:  Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult

Type: Utility

Use Phase to instantaneously teleport 3 SIZ per level from its current location to another place within 100’. Only complete objects can be teleported—it is impossible to teleport a part of a living being away. If an object is not directly affixed to another object or is being “used,” it can be teleported with no issues. The mage must be able to see the object targeted and the location it is to be teleported to.

For example, a dagger can be teleported out of a target’s sheath, but not the wheel out of a cart. If a living creature is holding the dagger, it gets an avoidance check.

For a living target, the target’s ACU is the opposing force. A willing target (such as oneself) does not require a resistance roll.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost. A fumble typically results in just a spell failure, but the SG could rule that something else happens, such as the mage being phased toward the item, the item flying off in a random direction, or maybe even phasing into the In-Between.

Spell Resisted: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Protection from Arrows
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 2 minutes per mana

Avoidance: Spirit

Special: Doubles duration

Type: Buff

Target becomes Difficult to hit with missile weapons. Each additional mana extends duration by 2 minutes or adds another protected target.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Protection from the Elements
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 10 minutes per mana

Avoidance: Spirit

Special: Doubles duration

Type: Buff

The target becomes immune to a specific element (fire, water, ice/cold, earth, air, or electricity) for 10 damage.

Each mana gives 10 minutes to duration and blocks an additional 10 damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Protection from Ravaging
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 30’

Target: 10’ sphere

Duration: 10 minutes per mana

Avoidance: Spellcraft

Special: Doubles duration

Type: Buff

Protection from Ravaging can be used in two ways.

It can create a 10’ sphere of protection blocks all effects of ravaging when used to create mana. This also negates the ravaging mage from gaining the mana from the area. This protects anything within the sphere. This does not protect from magic cast with ravaged mana.

Each mana level gives 10 minutes additional duration and sphere gains 5’.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Protection from the Never
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 3

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 30’

Target: Single object

Duration: 10 minutes per level

Avoidance: Spirit

Special: Doubles duration

Type: Buff

All in a 10’ radius of charmed object gain a +1 AV vs. Fey (Navirite) creatures, or protection from scrying through dreams. 

Each mana level gives 10 minutes additional duration and sphere gains 5’.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Push
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Agility or Effort

Special:

Type: Utility

A magical force pushes the object. This can be a person or an object. At mana level 1 it can cause Knockback against a target creature or knock over an object 5 lbs. or smaller.

Each additional mana can push a person or object 40 lbs. heavier 10’ backwards, doing 1d6 damage per mana. Avoidance with Agility or Effort stops the creature from being pushed, and they only take 1/2 damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Open
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: Varies

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Varies

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: None

Special: Doubles the range

Type: Utility

1 mana: You can open unlocked boxes, chests, and doors within 15’.

3 mana: You can open non-magical locked boxes, chests, and doors within 30’.

7 mana: You can open most magically locked boxes, chests, and doors within 50’. Anything locked by a magical ritual or artifact using spells of more than 7 mana will remain sealed.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Orrilot’s Constitution
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 2

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 minute

Avoidance: None

Special: Double the duration

Type: Buff

The target gains more Constitution. 

2 Mana: +10 to CON

4 Mana: +20 to CON

6 Mana: +30 to CON

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Resist
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Touch

Target: Single Target

Duration: 1 minute mana

Avoidance: Spirit

Special: Double Duration

Type: Buff

Target is imbued with a +5% bonus to any Save for the duration. Each mana level gives an additional 5% or an additional target.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Seal
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: 15 minutes per mana

Avoidance: Dodge

Special: Doubles the duration

Type: Utility

You magically seal the edges of two inanimate objects. These objects must not be in use, and fitted against one another. For example a door and a door frame, or a sword and this sheath. This bonds the two items as if one, and can not be separated unless by more powerful magic. Items may still be battered and broken, but the spell also gives them an additional 20 HP.

Each level adds either 15 minutes to the duration of the spell or adds +20 additional hit points to the hit points of the sealed objects. The caster may split levels between these two benefits, deciding how before casting the spell.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: See Magic
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Concentration

Avoidance: None

Special: Doubles the range.

Type: Utility

You see any magic items or spells active in the current range. The mana level determines the level of detail you have in your magic sight. The spell can not see through iron, one foot of stone, three feet of wood, or any other magical ways of hiding magic.

1 Mana: You will detect a faint aura in a general area where magic is.

2 Mana: The aura for magic becomes stronger, showing the exact area where magic is present.

3 Mana: With a Knowledge Arcana check the mage may learn some basic facts about the magic detected.

4 Mana: The same as the 2 mana version, except the spell lasts 1 hour without concentration.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shape Earth and Stone
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Varies

Special:

Type: Utility

You shape earth and/or stone into another form. Each mana level allows you to form a 5’x5’x5’ square of the material. If the material has already been crafted, shaped, or formed into an object, the spell becomes difficult. If the item is being used by a living being it becomes very difficult to reshape it.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shape Metal
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Varies

Special:

Type: Utility

You shape metal into another form. Each mana level allows you to form a 5’x5’x5’ square of the material. If the material has already been crafted, shaped, or formed into an object, the spell becomes difficult. If the item is being used by a living being it becomes very difficult to reshape it. Some metals are very difficult to form, and may require multiple checks and a crafting check to reshape.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shape Wood
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 60’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Varies

Special:

Type: Utility

You shape wood into another form. Each mana level allows you to form a 5’x5’x5’ square of the material. If the material has already been crafted, shaped, or formed into an object, the spell becomes difficult. If the item is being used by a living being it becomes very difficult to reshape it.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shelter
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 5’

Target: Single Space

Duration: 20 minutes

Avoidance: None

Special: Double the duration

Type: Utility

The mage creates a tent-like domed shelter that is 10’ wide and 5’ tall that protects the occupants from natural weather. Each additional mana gives an additional 5’ to each side and an additional 20 minutes.

The shelter can protect against temperatures from 0 degrees to 115 degrees, as well as snow to acidic rain. It will not protect from extreme events, like a magical ice storm or a rain of fiery lava.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shield
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 1

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: Self

Target: Self

Duration: 10 minutes per mana

Avoidance: None

Special: Doubles the duration

Type: Buff

You summon a shimmering shield that floats in front of you or is attached to your arm. It weights nothing and does not hinder spellcasting in any way. The magical shield gives you +2 AV and blocks any Mage Bolts directed at you. The shield has a total of 25 hp. Each additional mana doubles the duration and HP, but the AV is always 2. You may also charge the shield with elemental energy for 1 mana, giving bonuses based on the elemental focus you choose.

Any creature striking you with its body or a handheld weapon deals normal damage, but at the same time the attacker takes 1d6+1 points of elemental damage. Creatures wielding weapons with reach are not subject to this damage if they attack you.

The special powers of each version are as follows:

Fire Shield

The flames are warm to the touch. You take only half damage from ice-based attacks. You cast light for 30 ft. Successful attacks from water or ice-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Cold Shield

You take only half damage from fire-based attacks. Successful attacks from air-based or fire-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Water Shield

You take only half damage from fire, acid, and earth-based attacks. Successful attacks from air-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Electric Shield

You take only half damage from air and earth-based attacks. Successful attacks from water-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Earth Shield

You take only half damage from fire-based attacks. Successful attacks from air-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Air Shield

You take only half damage from air and electrical-based attacks. Successful attacks from earth-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Spirit Sap
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 3

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 30’

Target: Single Target

Duration: Instant

Avoidance: Spirit

Special:  Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult

Type: Debuff

You drain spirit from the target into the ether. The target loses 1d3 Spirit Points from the target for every 3 mana spent. 

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and doesn’t work.

item.title: Summon Minor Elemental
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Mana per Level: 2

Casting Time: 1 Standard Action

Range: 30’
