You have the authority and the jurisdiction to uphold and defend the law, ideally in defense of the common folk. Your enforcement of the law and legal code is supported by the power structure in your community.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, a medium weapon, and 1 contact. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 5 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Brawl, Dodge, Fast Talk, Knowledge (Law), Listen, Spot, and four of the following: Drive, First Aid, Grapple, Insight, Knowledge (Region or Group), Martial Arts, Melee Weapon Skill (Any), Ranged Weapons, Pilot (Any), Ride, Track.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Archeon, Fighter

Mage

You were training in one of the many ways of magic in the world. This does not grant you the ability to cast spells, only understand their base mechanics, their history, and their lore.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, a journal, ink and quill, and a small weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Fine Manipulation, Insight, Knowledge (Arcane), Persuade, Research, Sense, and Spellcraft, plus three other skills from Appraise, Etiquette, Listen, other Knowledge skills, Spot, and Teach.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Bard, Luminar, Wylder

Merchant

You make a living in trade, purchasing resources for less and selling for more. You might work in or own a shop, or you might be mobile: a traveling vendor, a wandering tinker, an adventuresome spice merchant, or an international traveler.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, and a small weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 20 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Appraise, Bargain, Fast Talk, Knowledge (Accounting), Knowledge (Business), Persuade, Research, and any two other skills as specialties: Command, Listen, Sense, and Spot
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Factor

Monster Hunter

Since the Return, the lands have been plagued with creatures who want nothing more than to harm those in Atheles. You are determined to stop them.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, leather armor, iron manacles, and a medium weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Brawl, Dodge, Grapple, Knowledge (*Monster Lore), Melee Weapon Skill (Any), Ranged Weapons, Survival and three other skills from the following list: Climb, Hide, Listen, Jump, Language (Other), Martial Arts, Ride, Sense, Shield, Spot, Stealth, Swim, Throw, Track.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Archeon, Ranger

*See Knowledge for how Knowledge Expertises work.

Mystic

You focus on the spiritual side of the world, often finding yourself in deep study or research. While you enjoy all knowledge you focus on the mystical. You can be a spiritual leader of a tribe, or a researcher in a library.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, and a small weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 20 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Insight, Knowledge (Arcane), Persuade, Research, Sense, Survival, and Spellcraft, plus three other skills from Appraise, First Aid, Listen, other Knowledge skills, Medicine,  Spot, and Teach.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths:Wylder or Factor

Noble

You were born into wealth and a ruling class. In a society that recognizes such social distinctions, your status is often associated with ranks and titles, but even if such titles are meaningless, you are still easily recognized as among the elite. You are accustomed to an elegant and extravagant lifestyle, and you know and are known to the wealthy and other highborn of society.

  • Starting: Fancy/fine clothing, a small weapon, and two contacts (one friendly, one adversarial). Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 40 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Bargain, Command, Drive, Etiquette, Literacy, and Strategy, plus any other three skills as hobbies or fields of interest.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Factor, Fighter

Occultist

You are a student of obscure secrets, hidden lore, and magical power, believing in the influence and primacy of supernatural forces.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, and a small weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 20 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Fast Talk, Insight, Knowledge (Arcane), Knowledge (History), Knowledge (Occult), Research, and any four other skills: Art (any), Craft (any), Knowledge (Archaeology), Medicine, Spellcraft.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Luminar, Wylder

Politician

You have been elected or appointed to a position of authority and make your living taking part in or directing government activities, whether city-wide or greater. You could be a medieval magistrate or a town mayor.

  • Starting: Fine clothing, a small weapon, and four contacts (two friendly, two adversarial). Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Bargain, Etiquette, Fast Talk, Insight, Knowledge (Law), Persuade, plus any four other skills: Command, Knowledge (Accounting, Group, History, or Region), Listen, Perform (Oratory), Research.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Any

Priest

Your faith and belief in the divine have led you to the calling of the priesthood, and you spend your time preaching or in activities befitting the worship of your deity (or deities). You could be an acetic monk or a church priest.

  • Starting: Basic or fine clothing, and two contacts. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Fast Talk, Insight, Knowledge (History), Knowledge (Philosophy), Knowledge (Religion), Perform, Persuade, and three of the following: Knowledge (Occult), Listen, Research, Spellcraft, Teach.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Luminar

Sailor

You ply the ocean waves, working hard to maintain your vessel’s integrity, whether as a pirate, a naval officer, or an ancient world trader.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, a light weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Climb, Dodge, Grapple, Navigate, Pilot (Boat), Swim, and any four of the following: Athletics, Acrobatics, Command, Language (Other), Listen, Melee Weapons (Any), Ranged Weapons, Repair,  Siege Weapons, Slashing Weapons, Spot.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Fighter, Rogue

Scholar

Study and learning define your life, and you specialize in one or more fields of knowledge, eagerly seeking out all there is to know within your intellectual domain. You might teach this knowledge to students or hoard it for your own use.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, a journal, ink and quill, and a contact. Funds: 1d4 x 5 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Persuade, Research, Teach, and choose five Knowledge or Craft skills appropriate to setting and related to your field of study.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Bard, Factor, Luminar

Servant

You are employed as a helper or servant, tending to the household affairs and domestic needs of an affluent employer. You might be a gentleman’s manservant, the adjutant of a general, or you could be a scullery maid in a sprawling castle.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, a small weapon. Funds (can vary): 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp (average) at character creation.
  • Skills: Craft (any), Etiquette, Hide, Listen, Stealth, and choose five of the following as personal specialties, as appropriate to the setting and concept: Bargain, Drive, First Aid, Insight, Knowledge (Accounting), Persuade.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Factor

Soldier

Whether through enlistment or conscription, you are a professional soldier, charged with the defense of your country or organization. You might be a mercenary or sellsword, or you might actually believe in the cause you fight for. Either way, you’ve received martial training, and work in a squad of other soldiers, doing what you’re ordered to.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, leather armor, an a medium weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Brawl, Climb, Dodge, First Aid, and six of the following: Command,Grapple, Hide, Listen, Jump, Medicine, Melee Weapon Skill (Any), Ranged Weapons, Navigate, Repair, Ride, Siege Weapon, Spot, Stealth, Throw
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Berserker, Fighter

Spy

You are skilled in subterfuge and infiltration, entering countries or organizations under cover or in secret, finding out their secrets, and reporting them back to an enemy or interested party.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, a small weapon, and a contact. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 20 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Dodge, Fast Talk, Hide, Listen, Research, Spot, Stealth, and three of the following: Brawl, Disguise, Etiquette, Grapple, Knowledge (any), Martial Arts, Navigate, Pilot (any), Repair (Mechanical), Ranged Weapons, Ride, Swim, Throw, Track.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Rogue

Thief

You take what you want, either to survive or because it appeals to you. You could be a lowly pickpocket, an infamous brigand, or a glamorous international jewel thief. You flaunt the law to steal, either armed, through clever deception, stealth, or some combination of all three.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, a small weapon, and a contact. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 20 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Appraise, Dodge, Fast Talk, Hide, Stealth, and five other skills from the following list, as appropriate to setting and concept: Bargain, Brawl, Climb, Disguise, Fine Manipulation, Grapple, Insight, Listen, Jump, Knowledge (Law), Persuade, Repair (Mechanical), Spot, and any Weapon.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Rogue

Warrior

You specialize in individual combat, surviving by your reflexes and your weapon skills. Perhaps you adhere to a code of honor like chivalry or bushido, or you are a mercenary or barbaric adventurer. Soldiers fight in groups or squads, while warriors look only to their own prowess.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, leather armor, and a medium weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Brawl, Dodge, Grapple, Melee Weapon Skill (Any), Ranged Weapons, and five other skills from the following list: Athletics, Climb, Hide, Listen, Jump, Martial Arts, Ride, Shield, Spot, Stealth, Swim, Throw, Track.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Berserker, Fighter

Witchhunter

You are training in hunting mages, whether you find heretics who offended the church or a necromancer who defiles the graves.

  • Starting: Basic clothing, leather armor, iron manacles, and a medium weapon. Funds: 1d4 x 10 + 10 gp at character creation.
  • Skills: Brawl, Dodge, Grapple, Melee Weapon Skill (Any), Ranged Weapons, Spellcraft, Survival and three other skills from the following list: Climb, Hide, Listen, Jump, Language (Other), Martial Arts, Ride, Sense, Shield, Spot, Stealth, Swim, Throw, Track.
  • Recommended Archetype Talent Paths: Archeon

Finishing Touches

You can examine your character sheet and reflect on the choices you’ve made during character creation. Perhaps some of the skills indicate a personality type, such as studious, eclectic, violent, or some other such adjective. You might try to find the character’s ‘voice’, or some other means of expression that makes them come alive at the gaming table. Not everyone wants to be a method actor, though, and don’t feel that you need to come to the table with an in-depth personality and back-story for your character. Some of that will come during the roleplaying experience, and some of it will come naturally as your character is faced with adventure and adversity.

The hard part is over—now the fun can begin!

item.title: Point Based Characteristic Calculator
item.dc:creator: zerosrd

item.title: SagaBorn d100 Encounter Calculator
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Enter Talent Point Level (TPL) for each hero (up to 6):

Select encounter difficulty:



📊 Results

📖 CV & Tier Reference
  • Minion: –0.5 CV (½ HP, no Dodge/Parry)
  • Grunt: Base CV
  • Champion: +0.5 CV (2× HP, 65% Dodge, Resistant)
  • Boss: +1.0 CV (3× HP, 75% Dodge, Resistant)

How to Use the SagaBorn Encounter Calculator

This tool helps StoryGuides quickly build balanced combat encounters using the SagaBorn BRP system. To use it:

  1. Enter the Talent Point Level (TPL) for each hero in the party. Most starting characters begin with 3 TPL and gain 1 point per story arc.
  2. Select your desired difficulty from the dropdown. This will scale the encounter to be easy, balanced, or deadly based on the party’s combined TPL (used here as Combat Value, or CV).
  3. Click "Calculate Encounter CV" to see the total party CV and the recommended enemy CV budget.
  4. Use that number to choose a mix of monsters from the Creature Compendium. Combine Minions, Grunts, Champions, and Bosses until you match the budget.
    • Remember: Champions count as +0.5 CV, and Bosses as +1 CV, due to their added defenses and health.

item.title: Aid
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles Bonus
  • Type: Buff

The target gains +10 per mana spent on a chosen skill until that skill is used.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and the mage loses the mana spent.

item.title: Animal Friend
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute per mana
  • Avoidance: Spirit
  • Special:
  • Type: Utility

You may use Persuasion with animals. Each level of mana allows you +10% to your Persuasion skill when dealing with animals. The communication between the mage and the animal is simple, with emotions or simple directions from the caster being understood by the animal. The caster can also gain simple understanding of the animal's wants or needs.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Babble
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Concentration
  • Avoidance: Spirit
  • Special: Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult
  • Type: Mind

Babble causes the target only to be able to make incoherent noises and sounds. To themselves, they sound as if they are speaking normally, but others can not understand the strange noises they are making. The more mana imbued in the spell, the more serious the babbling becomes. The spell lasts as long as the spellcaster concentrates on it, though the target gets to attempt to save each round on their turn. Any mana level of babble causes all communication and spellcasting by the target to become Difficult.

1 Mana: The target's words become incoherent.

2 Mana: The target can not stop speaking, feeling compelled to talk about anything while no one understands them.

3 Mana: The target is frantically babbling, not even making sense to themselves, and all actions are considered Difficult.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Bear’s Strength
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 2
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: None
  • Type: Buff

The target becomes stronger, gaining magical strength. 

2 Mana: +10 to STR

4 Mana: +20 to STR

6 Mana: +30 to STR

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Befriend
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 30’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 Hour per ACU
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spirit vs. Spirit
  • Special: Double Duration
  • Type: Mind

The target sees the spellcaster as a friend. The target will engage with them in a friendly manner, but will not do anything that would harm themselves or others they consider allies. Attempts to Persuade them become Easy, unless it is dangerous or against their beliefs. They are still fully capable of their own actions and thoughts and lose no memories of past interactions with the spellcaster or their allies.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and the mana is lost.

item.title: Bend Sound
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Varies
  • Target: Varies
  • Duration: 1 round per level
  • Avoidance: Agility for half unless otherwise stated
  • Special: Double damage. Critical: Double damage maxxed.
  • Type: Damage

Mana 1: You produce a loud, piercing sound that can break fragile, non-magical items, temporarily deafen an opponent, or harm creatures made of crystal.

When used in an area, this effect shatters non-magical objects made of crystal, glass, ceramic, or porcelain within a 5-foot radius. These objects are broken into pieces unless they weigh more than one pound per your caster level. All exposed fragile or crystalline objects and crystalline creatures also take 1d6 sonic damage.

You can also deafen one target for 2d4 rounds with a successful attack. The target can attempt a Fortitude save to halve the duration.

Mana 3: You can manipulate sound to counter and dispel a Silence spell by making a Heroic action against the opposing caster's Spellcraft skill.

Mana 5: You unleash a powerful blast of sound across a 30’ area, causing each creature in it to suffer 3d8 sonic damage. A successful Stamina save is required to avoid being stunned for 1 round. Creatures that are deaf cannot be stunned but still take damage. All exposed fragile or crystalline objects and crystalline creatures also take 1d6 sonic damage.This effect does not penetrate a Silence spell; to bypass it, the Silence spell must be dispelled first.

Mana 7: You release a devastating, high-pitched scream that deafens and injures all creatures in its 60’ cone range. Affected creatures are deafened for 2d6 rounds and take 5d6 sonic damage, with a Spirit save to resist deafness and Agility to halve the damage. All exposed fragile or crystalline objects and crystalline creatures also take 1d6 sonic damage. This effect also cannot penetrate a Silence spell and requires dispelling the silence to take effect fully.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and the mana is lost.

item.title: Blur
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Double Duration
  • Type: Buff

4 mana: The spell’s target shifts in this plane, making them harder to see and touch. Any attack against the target becomes difficult.

7 mana: The spell’s target becomes difficult to see, making attacks against them difficult, as well as allowing the blurred creature to move through most solid objects. Iron or steel block blurred creatures.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and mana is lost.

item.title: Cat’s Dexterity
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 2
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special:
  • Type: Buff

The target becomes more agile, gaining magical speed. 

2 Mana: +10 to DEX

4 Mana: +20 to DEX

6 Mana: +30 to DEX

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and mana is lost.

item.title: Command
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spirit vs. Spirit
  • Special:  Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult
  • Type: Mind

You issue a command to a creature, compelling them to obey, whether it's simple or complex. The complexity of the command affects the mana cost, as does the duration required for the creature to complete the task. The spell has no effect on undead or creatures that do not understand your language. It cannot force a creature to kill itself or engage in actions that would guarantee its death, but it can command nearly any other course of action.

  • 1 Mana: A single-word command, such as "drop," "halt," or "flee." The target follows the command on its next action and can do nothing else for one round.
  • 3 Mana: A simple command like "pick up that sword" or "don’t harm him." The target follows the comm
  • and on its next action and does nothing else for one round per caster level.
  • 5 Mana: A more detailed command, such as "throw all your weapons into the river" or "stop fighting and behave." The target obeys on its next action and continues for 1 minute per mana spent.
  • 7 Mana: A complex directive, requiring the target to perform a service or refrain from an action as specified. The target begins obeying on its next action and must continue for one day per caster level or until the task is completed.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and mana is lost.

Spell avoided: Target becomes dazed for rounds equal to mana spent.

item.title: Conjure Creature
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Empty Space
  • Duration: 2 rounds per mana
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Creature gains +2 damage
  • Type: Summoning

This spell draws the consciousness of a creature from the Navirim and places it within a body made of the caster’s mana. The summoned creature appears at the chosen location and acts immediately, taking its turn along with the caster. It fights for you with its full abilities.

By using more mana, the caster can summon stronger creatures.

Examples of Summoned Creatures:

The summoned beings are not true living creatures but projections of the caster's power, each with similar abilities to real creatures. They may appear in different forms, reflecting the caster's personality or desires, and can take on the form of elemental creatures if the caster chooses.

Common Creature Options: Imp (Demon), Wolf, Fox, Large Cat

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and mana is lost.

item.title: Countermagic
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: Reaction
  • Range: 100’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spellcraft vs. Spellcraft
  • Special: None
  • Type: Debuff

You may cast Countermagic as a reaction to interrupt another caster’s spell. State your intention to cast Countermagic and the amount of mana you’ll use before learning the opponent’s spell mana cost. Both casters roll a Heroic Action using their Spellcraft skill, with the higher successful roll prevailing. Countermagic disrupts the magical energy of the targets spell according to the mana spent on Countermagic. If the mana spent on counter magic meets or exceeds the amount spent on the original casting, then the targeted spell is canceled. If it is less, this will lower the power of the targeted spell equal to the Countermagic spent mana.

For example, Mingus casts Elemental Blast. Tersh reacts with Countermagic. Tersh declares the are spending 4 mana and they roll a 56 Spellcraft. Mingus rolls their Spellcraft to contest the Countermagic, but they only roll a 34. Tersh wins the contest, but Mingus had spent 6 mana on their Elemental Blast. Tersh’s Countermagic cancels 4 mana worth of the Elemental Blast, causing it to only be cast at a 2 mana level, though Mingus still spends 6 mana.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

Spell avoided: The spell fails, and the opposing caster still expends their mana.

item.title: Creeping Element
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: N/A
  • Special: Double Damage
  • Type: Damage

A chosen element forms around the target, seeping into their skin doing elemental damage.

Each mana level does 1d4+1 damage plus its elemental bonus. Creeping element ignores the AV of the target creature.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires or dilutes acid. Water can also choke the target, causing it to become Panicked. A successful Spirit check to resists this fear.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Effort save to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures and causing blindness for 1 round. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air chokes the target, causing it to become Panicked. A successful Spirit check to resists this fear.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Cure Disease
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 hour
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: N/A
  • Special: Double the bonus
  • Type: Healing

Cure disease gives the target a +10% to their Stamina the next time they save versus a disease. For each mana level spent, the spell adds an additional +10% to the save.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Dark
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Dodge
  • Special:
  • Type: Utility

This spell causes an object to radiate shadowy illumination out to a 20’ radius (every level expands it by another 20’). All creatures in the area become concealed, making any actions towards them Difficult . Even creatures that can normally see in such conditions (such as with dark vision) have the miss chance in an area shrouded in magical darkness.

Normal lights (torches, candles, lanterns, and so forth) are incapable of brightening the area, as are light spells of lower level.

If Dark is cast on a small object that is then placed inside or under a light-proof covering, the spell’s effect is blocked until the covering is removed.

Darkness counters and dispels a Light spell (one with the light descriptor) by a Heroic action vs the opposing mage using the Spellcraft Skill.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Dispel
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level:1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 100’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: None
  • Type: Utility

A mage can use dispel to rid themselves or another target of the effects of a spell. Unlike Countermagic, it must overcome the mana of the spell targeted, but unlike Countermagic, there is no Heroic Action to negate the spell. Dispel must be targeted at a single spell, and can not effect multiple spells even if they effect the same target. The casting mage must be aware of the spell to target it. Dispel may also be used against summoned creatures or magical traps.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Acid Bolt
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Dodge, Parry*
  • Special: Bleeding
  • Type: Damage

A bolt of acid flies from the mage’s outstretched hand toward the target. Acid bolt does 1d4+1 damage per mana spent plus acts as an Acid, causing 1 AV damage for 1d4 rounds. A mage must successfully use Spot to hit the target.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and the mage loses the mana spent.

item.title: Acid Fog
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: AoE 5’ Radius Sphere per mana level
  • Duration: 1d4 rounds per mana level
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Double Duration, Critical: Max Damage and Duration
  • Type: Damage

A dense fog of corrosive acid coalesces in the area. The acid does 1d4 damage to creatures and other exposed objects. The acid also reduces Armor Value of any armor worn by creatures in this area by 1 per round. The acid does initial damage as the spell is cast, and then does damage to creatures still in the fog at the end of their turn. Inanimate objects not carried or wielded by creatures take damage at the end of the turn. 

Targeting: A caster must target a single square and then successfully roll a Spot skill check to hit that target. If the Spot skill fails, roll 1d8, and the spell lands in an adjoining space. To determine the direction, the closest space to the spellcaster being 1 and rotating clockwise. To determine how far from the intended target space, it is equal to 5’x the spells damage dice away from the target space. On a fumble (100), the spell centers on the caster. For more see Targeting AoE Spells previously in this chapter.

The only way to avoid the acid fog is to leave the affected area. Water or bases can dilute the acid, causing half damage. Once out of the fog, the acid dissipates from any person or object.

Spellcraft failure: The cloud fails to materialize and the mage loses the mana spent..

Spellcraft fumble: the cloud centers on caster.

item.title: Elemental Arrow
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Varies
  • Target: Single or MultipleTarget
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Dodge
  • Special: Impaling; Critical: Max Damage
  • Type: Damage

A magical arrow of force or a chosen energy type (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) springs from your hand and speeds to its target. An arrow is considered magical damage or elemental damage. A mage must successfully use Aim to hit the target with each arrow. You cast a separate arrow for each mana spent, and you may direct each arrow at a different target. Arrows must all be of a matching elemental energy. 

1 Mana: 1d8+3 Damage 60 ft. Range.

Each additional level adds another arrow, allowing for multiple targets (one per arrow), and 60’ to the range.

If the spell is imbued with an element, the spell also causes the Elemental Effect to any target that it does damage to.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires or dilute acid.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Effort save to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air knocks SIZ 20 or smaller creatures prone. Agility save to negate falling.

Spellcraft failure: Your arrow misses its target. For a fumble, use the Missle Fumble Chart.

item.title: Elemental Blast
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 2
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Varies
  • Target: AoE 5’ Radius Sphere per mana level
  • Duration: 1 minute per mana
  • Avoidance: Dodge for half
  • Special: Dazes; Critical: Max Damage
  • Type: Damage

A small ball of energy emits from your hand traveling to an intended target area and bursts into a spherical blast of elemental energy. You can choose any elemental energy (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) available to you that you have control over, or it can be a generic magical blast. 

2 Mana: 1d6+1 damage, 5 ft. radius sphere, 30 ft. range. 

Each additional level adds 1d6+1 damage, 5’ to the radius sphere, and 30’ to the range.

Targeting: A caster must target a single square and then successfully roll a Spot skill check to hit that target. If the Spot skill fails, roll 1d8, and the spell lands in an adjoining space. To determine the direction, the closest space to the spellcaster being 1 and rotating clockwise. To determine how far from the intended target space, it is equal to 5’x the spells damage dice away from the target space. On a fumble (100), the spell centers on the caster. For more see Targeting AoE Spells previously in this chapter.

If the spell is imbued with an element, the spell also causes the Elemental Effect to any target that it does damage to.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires or dilute acid.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Effort save to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air knocks SIZ 20 or smaller creatures prone. Agility save to negate falling.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles.

item.title: Elemental Shield
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Self
  • Target: Self
  • Duration: 3 rounds per mana level
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: see below
  • Type: Damage

This spell wreaths you in magical or elemental energy (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) and causes damage to each creature that attacks you in melee.

Any creature striking you with its body or a handheld weapon deals normal damage, but at the same time the attacker takes 1d6+1 points of damage. Creatures wielding weapons with reach are not subject to this damage if they attack you. The shield gains 1d6+1 damage per mana level.

The special powers of each version are as follows:

Magical Force

An invisible shield surrounds you doing the spells standard damage to any that hit you.

Fire Shield

The flames are warm to the touch. You take only half damage from ice-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. You cast light for 30 ft. Successful attacks from water or ice-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Cold Shield

The shimmering shield is cool to the touch. You take only half damage from fire-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. 

Water Shield

This liquid shield flows over your body. You take only half damage from fire and earth-based attacks, as well as diluting acid attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save.

Electric Shield

Arcs of lightning crisscross your body. You take only half damage from air and earth-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. Successful attacks from water-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Earth Shield

Rough earth covers your body. You take only half damage from fire-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. 

Air Shield

An invisible barrier covers you. You take only half damage from air and electrical-based attacks. If such an attack allows a Reflex save for half damage, you take no damage on a successful save. Successful attacks from earth-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Spell Failure: You lose any mana spent on conjuring the shield.

item.title: Elemental Storm
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 2
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Varies
  • Target: AoE Radius Sphere per mana level
  • Duration: 1d4 rounds
  • Avoidance: Dodge for half
  • Special: Doubles the duration, Critical: Max damage
  • Type: Damage

A magical force or a chosen element (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) rains down from the sky in the area of effect. This spells needs space above the ground with the sky above (meaning it can not be cast inside rooms or cramped dungeons).

2 Mana: 1d4+1 damage, 10 ft. radius sphere, 50 ft. range. 

Each additional level adds 1d4+1 damage, 10’ to the radius sphere, and 15’ to the range.

2 Mana: 1d4+1 damage, 10 ft. radius sphere, 50 ft. range. 

Each additional level adds 1d4+1 damage, 10’ to the radius sphere, and 15’ to the range.

Targeting: A caster must target a single square and then successfully roll a Spot skill check to hit that target. If the Spot skill fails, roll 1d8, and the spell lands in an adjoining space. To determine the direction, the closest space to the spellcaster being 1 and rotating clockwise. To determine how far from the intended target space, it is equal to 5’x the spells damage dice away from the target space. On a fumble (100), the spell centers on the caster. For more see Targeting AoE Spells previously in this chapter.

If the spell is imbued with an element, the spell also causes the Elemental Effect to any target that it does damage to.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires or dilute acid.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2. Effort save to negate. 
  • Earth covers the objects in dust and dirt, outlining any invisible objects or creatures. 
  • Electricity does an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air knocks SIZ 20 or smaller creatures prone. Agility save to negate falling.

Spellcraft failure: Roll 1d8, and the spell lands in the adjoining space from the target space, with the closest space to the spellcaster being 1 and rotating clockwise. For a fumble, the spell centers on the caster.

Spellcraft Fumble: The spell targets the caster.

item.title: Elemental Sphere
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 100’
  • Target: 5 ft. diameter sphere
  • Duration: 2 rounds per mana
  • Avoidance: Dodge for half
  • Special: Double damage, Critical: Max damage
  • Type: Damage

A globe of elemental energy rolls in whichever direction you point, and strikes a target. It moves 30 ft. per round. If it enters a space with a creature, it stops moving for the round and deals an effect based on the elemental type. An Elemental Sphere rolls over barriers less than 4 ft. tall. You can choose any elemental energy (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) available to you that you have control over.

An Elemental Sphere gains the following powers based on the type of element.

  • Fire causes flammable items to catch fire and burn. Low melting point metals melt. Target gains the On Fire effect.
  • Water puts out mundane fires and does 3d6 damage to creatures of the elemental type fire.
  • Ice slows creatures by 1/2 and does 2d6 cold damage. 
  • Earthen spikes impale the target for 2d6 damage. Caster may try to surround and grapple the target with the Sphere with a successful Heroic action (Intellect vs target Agility). If successful, the target is grappled and the sphere can not move until the target is let loose.
  • Electricity does 2d6 damage, with an extra 1d4 damage to anyone in heavy metal armor (chainmail, breastplate, half plate, or full plate).
  • Air causes a Medium or smaller target to lift into the air 10’ and crash down for 1d6 damage. The target can be moved up to 10 ft. and lands prone. This increases by 10’ and 1d6 damage per mana.

The Elemental Sphere moves as long as you actively direct it (Concentration); otherwise, it merely stays at rest. The surface of the Sphere has a soft, yielding consistency and so does not cause damage except by its elemental energy. It cannot push aside unwilling creatures or batter down large obstacles. An Elemental Sphere winks out if it exceeds the spell’s range. Each mana level gives the effect +1d6.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Elemental Wall
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 min per mana
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: None
  • Type: Damage

You conjure a wall of elemental energy to block a path or create a protective ring around yourself. Choose an element you can control (fire, water, earth, air, ice, or electricity) for the wall’s energy.

  • Mana 1: A 10 ft. long, 10 ft. tall, and 1 ft. thick wall dealing 1d4+1 damage to anyone passing through it.
  • Mana 3: A 20 ft. long, 10 ft. tall, and 1 ft. thick wall dealing 3d4+3 damage to anyone passing through it.
  • Mana 5: A 40 ft. long, 10 ft. tall, and 1 ft. thick wall dealing 3d6+3 damage to anyone passing through. The wall can encircle the caster with a 5 ft. radius and can be positioned off-center, allowing space for other medium or small creatures.
  • Mana 7: A 50 ft. long, 10 ft. tall, and 1 ft. thick wall dealing 5d6+5 damage to anyone passing through. The wall can encircle the caster with a 10 ft. radius, with room for medium or small creatures if positioned off-center. Additionally, the caster may cause one side of the wall to emit energy, dealing 1d4+1 damage to anyone within 10 ft

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Fear
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Varies
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spirit vs. Spirit
  • Special: Doubles Duration
  • Type: Mind

You cause fear to creatures based on the mana spent. 

  • Mana 1: With a successful Spellcraft, a single target becomes Shaken.
  • Mana 3: With a successful Spellcraft, a single target becomes Scared.
  • Mana 5: With a successful Spellcraft, up to three creatures become Scared.
  • Mana 7: With a successful Spellcraft, up to three creatures become Panicked.

After the initial round, a Scared or Panicked creature may roll a Spirit check Vs the spellcaster’s Spellcraft skill. With a successful save, the target recovers and only becomes Shaken for the duration of the spell.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Find
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana: 5
  • Casting Time: 1 hour
  • Range: 15 miles
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: None
  • Type: Utility

This spell helps you locate a person or object familiar to you. As you turn, you sense when you are facing the direction of the target, provided it is within range. If the target is moving, you also know its direction of travel.

The spell can find a specific creature, a known item, or an item of a particular kind. However, it cannot locate creatures of a general type. To locate a kind of creature, you must have seen one up close (within 30 feet) at least once.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Fox’s Fascination
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 2
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Double the duration
  • Type: Buff

The target becomes more charismatic. 

  • 2 Mana: +10 to SOC
  • 4 Mana: +20 to SOC
  • 6 Mana: +30 to SOC

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Gain Spirit
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles spirit
  • Type: Buff

The target recovers 1 lost Spirit Point per 3 mana spent.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and doesn’t work.

item.title: Heal
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Dodge
  • Special: Add 1d4 healing
  • Type: Healing

When laying your hand upon a living creature, you channel energy that cures the target.

1 Mana: heals 1d6 +3

Each additional mana adds 1d6+3

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Identify
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 20 Minutes
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special:
  • Type: Utility

The spell reveals all magical properties of a single item, including activation methods (if applicable) and any remaining charges. When used on an artifact, Identify may not yield complete information but can offer hints about the artifact's abilities.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Illusionary Image
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 300’
  • Target: Empty space
  • Duration: Concentration
  • Avoidance: Intellect
  • Special:  Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult
  • Type: Mind

This spell creates the visual illusion of an object, creature, or force, as visualized by you. 

  • Mana 1: The illusion can be seen, but lacks sound, smell, texture, or temperature.
  • Mana 3: The illusion can be seen and has minor sounds, but not speech, smell, texture, or temperature.
  • Mana 5: The illusion can be seen and has full speech, smell, and temperature.
  • Mana 7: The illusion can be seen and has full speech, smell, texture, and temperature.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Imbue Staff
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 2 minutes
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Self
  • Duration: 4 hours
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles duration
  • Type: Buff

Preferred by the druids of the Druid Council, this causes their weapon to become a powerful way to subdue an enemy without lethal force.

  • 1 Mana: The spellcaster’s staff gains +5% to hit and +1d4 subdual damage.
  • 3 Mana: The spellcaster’s staff gains +10% to hit and +2d4 subdual damage.
  • 5 Mana: The spellcaster’s staff gains +15% to hit and +3d4 subdual damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Imbue Weapon
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single weapon
  • Duration: 2 minutes
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles duration
  • Type: Buff

The target's weapon becomes more lethal.

  • 1 Mana: The weapon gains +5% to hit and +1 damage.
  • 3 Mana: The weapon gains +10% to hit and +2 damage.
  • 5 Mana: The weapon gains +15% to hit and +3 damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Invulnerable Sphere
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target or Space
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles duration and absorption
  • Type: Buff

The mage creates an invulnerable sphere that absorbs damage, protecting anyone inside. With a single mana worth of energy, the sphere is 5’ in diameter and absorbs 5 hit points worth of damage. For each mana level beyond the first, it can be expanded another 5’ or abosob another 5 damage. This does not block any objects coming out of the protected sphere, nor does it protects against non damaging spells.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Levitate
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 30’ per mana
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 2 minutes per mana
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spirit vs. Spirit
  • Special:
  • Type: Utility

Levitate makes a creature or object levitate. It takes one mana per 10 SIZ to levitate. The target may be raised or lowered 30’ per mana each round. The spellcaster most concentrate on this task. While the spellcaster can not move the target horizontally, the target may pull itself along a surface, such as a wall, to also change their position as they rise or lower.

Attacking while levitating is Difficult. If the spell ends the target floats slowly to the ground.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Light
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Creatures or objects
  • Duration: Varies
  • Avoidance: Varies
  • Special: None, Critical: Max Damage
  • Type: Utility

Mana 1: Light will cause a single object to glow like a torch, lighting up an area of 30’. Light taken into an area of magical darkness does not function. It lasts 1 hour.

Mana 2: You create up to four lights that resemble lanterns or torches (and cast that amount of light), or up to four glowing spheres of light (which look like will-o’-wisps), or one faintly glowing, vaguely creature-like shape. They must stay within 10 ft. of each other. The lights move at 100 ft. per round. You can use these lights to blind or distract a foe giving them a -5% to all skills until the light spell is canceled or the caster stops concentrating. These lights can also be used to outline hidden foes. Outlined creatures do not benefit from the concealment normally provided by darkness-based effects, Blur, Displacement, Invisibility, or similar effects.

Mana 3: You may enchant an item that sheds light as bright as full daylight in a 60 ft. radius, and dim light for an additional 60 ft. beyond that. Creatures that take penalties in bright light also take them while within the radius of this magical light. This is not the equivalent of daylight for the purposes of creatures that are damaged or destroyed by bright light. A mana 3 (or above) Light spell counters and dispels a Darkness spell (one with the darkness descriptor) by a Heroic action vs the opposing mage using the Spellcraft Skill.

If cast on a small object that is then placed inside or under a light-proof covering, the spell’s effects are blocked until the covering is removed.

Mana 5: Focusing light like a ray of the sun, you project a blast of light from your open palm. A creature struck takes 2d8 points of damage. An undead creature takes an additional 2d8 damage.

Mana 7: A 10 ft. glowing ball of light energy comes into existence casting light as bright as the sun. You may summon this within the 100 ft. range of the spell, and the light fills a 300 ft. radius. Any within 40 ft. of the sphere take 3d8 damage. Undead take 6d8 damage. This is the equivalent of daylight for the purposes of creatures that are damaged or destroyed by bright light.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Mage Bolt
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target per bolt
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles Damage. Critical: Double Damage Maxed.
  • Type: Damage

A bolt of magical energy darts forth from your fingertip and strikes its target, dealing 1d4+1 points of force magical damage. The bolt cannot miss if the spellcaster can see even a small part of the target. Mage Bolt can not be dodged. Since it is magical damage, it bypasses most mundane AV. A Mage Bolt is always considered aimed, but specific areas of the target can not be singled out. 

For each mana level of the spell, the mage adds another Mage Bolt. Individual bolts may target different creatures or objects.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Mage Compass
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Adds +2 to the bonus. Critical: Doubles the bonus.
  • Type: Utility

Mage compass bestows directional sense to it’s target. This gives a 5% bonus to the spell target in Navigation skill checks per mana level. 

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Phantasmal Hands
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Concentration (Max: 10 min per level)
  • Avoidance: See below
  • Special: Double Duration; Critical Triple Duration
  • Type: Utility

A spectral version of your hands appear and can be controlled by the mage. They can be used to pick up, move, Grapple, Brawl, or manipulate items. The hands can can be used in the same round as they are cast. Each round after, they can be controlled as a free action.

Pick up or move:  Inanimate objects can be freely grabbed or moved with phantasmal hands. Objects being used or protected can be grabbed with a Heroic Action.

Manipulate Items: The hands can open doors, lift treasure chest lids, or other things normal hands can do. Anything that requires Fine Manipulation is difficult though.

Grapple: The hands can be used to Grapple a creature. This is a contested Grapple check. If the hands are already grappling a creature, they can be used to move the creature, subdue the creature (causing it to be unable to have Standard Actions), or crush the creature for 1d6+1 damage.

Pummel: The hands can be used to pummel a target with the Brawl skill fopr 1d6+1 damage. Each extra mana level does 1d4+1 damage.

1 mana: You summon phantasmal hands which can MOV at 25’ per round, lift or Grapple anything SIZ 2 (30 lbs.) or smaller, or pummel for 1d6+1 damage. Each level adds an extra 5’ to the MOV, doubles the weight, and adds 1d4 to the damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Phase
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spirit vs. Spirit
  • Special:  Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult
  • Type: Utility

Use Phase to instantaneously teleport 3 SIZ per level from its current location to another place within 100’. Only complete objects can be teleported—it is impossible to teleport a part of a living being away. If an object is not directly affixed to another object or is being “used,” it can be teleported with no issues. The mage must be able to see the object targeted and the location it is to be teleported to.

For example, a dagger can be teleported out of a target’s sheath, but not the wheel out of a cart. If a living creature is holding the dagger, it gets an avoidance check.

For a living target, the target’s ACU is the opposing force. A willing target (such as oneself) does not require a resistance roll.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost. A fumble typically results in just a spell failure, but the SG could rule that something else happens, such as the mage being phased toward the item, the item flying off in a random direction, or maybe even phasing into the In-Between.

Spell Resisted: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Protection from Arrows
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 2 minutes per mana
  • Avoidance: Spirit
  • Special: Doubles duration
  • Type: Buff

Target becomes Difficult to hit with missile weapons. Each additional mana extends duration by 2 minutes or adds another protected target.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Protection from the Elements
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 10 minutes per mana
  • Avoidance: Spirit
  • Special: Doubles duration
  • Type: Buff

The target becomes immune to a specific element (fire, water, ice/cold, earth, air, or electricity) for 10 damage.

Each mana gives 10 minutes to duration and blocks an additional 10 damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Protection from Ravaging
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 30’
  • Target: 10’ sphere
  • Duration: 10 minutes per mana
  • Avoidance: Spellcraft
  • Special: Doubles duration
  • Type: Buff

Protection from Ravaging can be used in two ways.

It can create a 10’ sphere of protection blocks all effects of ravaging when used to create mana. This also negates the ravaging mage from gaining the mana from the area. This protects anything within the sphere. This does not protect from magic cast with ravaged mana.

Each mana level gives 10 minutes additional duration and sphere gains 5’.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Protection from the Never
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 30’
  • Target: Single object
  • Duration: 10 minutes per level
  • Avoidance: Spirit
  • Special: Doubles duration
  • Type: Buff

All in a 10’ radius of charmed object gain a +1 AV vs. Fey (Navirite) creatures, or protection from scrying through dreams. 

Each mana level gives 10 minutes additional duration and sphere gains 5’.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Push
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Agility or Effort
  • Special:
  • Type: Utility

A magical force pushes the object. This can be a person or an object. At mana level 1 it can cause Knockback against a target creature or knock over an object 5 lbs. or smaller.

Each additional mana can push a person or object 40 lbs. heavier 10’ backwards, doing 1d6 damage per mana. Avoidance with Agility or Effort stops the creature from being pushed, and they only take 1/2 damage.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Open
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: Varies
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Varies
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles the range
  • Type: Utility

1 mana: You can open unlocked boxes, chests, and doors within 15’.

3 mana: You can open non-magical locked boxes, chests, and doors within 30’.

7 mana: You can open most magically locked boxes, chests, and doors within 50’. Anything locked by a magical ritual or artifact using spells of more than 7 mana will remain sealed.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Orrilot’s Constitution
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 2
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Double the duration
  • Type: Buff

The target gains more Constitution. 

  • 2 Mana: +10 to CON
  • 4 Mana: +20 to CON
  • 6 Mana: +30 to CON

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Resist
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute mana
  • Avoidance: Spirit
  • Special: Double Duration
  • Type: Buff

Target is imbued with a +5% bonus to any Save for the duration. Each mana level gives an additional 5% or an additional target.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Seal
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 15 minutes per mana
  • Avoidance: Dodge
  • Special: Doubles the duration
  • Type: Utility

You magically seal the edges of two inanimate objects. These objects must not be in use, and fitted against one another. For example a door and a door frame, or a sword and this sheath. This bonds the two items as if one, and can not be separated unless by more powerful magic. Items may still be battered and broken, but the spell also gives them an additional 20 HP.

Each level adds either 15 minutes to the duration of the spell or adds +20 additional hit points to the hit points of the sealed objects. The caster may split levels between these two benefits, deciding how before casting the spell.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: See Magic
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Concentration
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles the range.
  • Type: Utility

You see any magic items or spells active in the current range. The mana level determines the level of detail you have in your magic sight. The spell can not see through iron, one foot of stone, three feet of wood, or any other magical ways of hiding magic.

  • 1 Mana: You will detect a faint aura in a general area where magic is.
  • 2 Mana: The aura for magic becomes stronger, showing the exact area where magic is present.
  • 3 Mana: With a Knowledge Arcana check the mage may learn some basic facts about the magic detected.
  • 4 Mana: The same as the 2 mana version, except the spell lasts 1 hour without concentration.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shape Earth and Stone
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Varies
  • Special:
  • Type: Utility

You shape earth and/or stone into another form. Each mana level allows you to form a 5’x5’x5’ square of the material. If the material has already been crafted, shaped, or formed into an object, the spell becomes difficult. If the item is being used by a living being it becomes very difficult to reshape it.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shape Metal
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Varies
  • Special:
  • Type: Utility

You shape metal into another form. Each mana level allows you to form a 5’x5’x5’ square of the material. If the material has already been crafted, shaped, or formed into an object, the spell becomes difficult. If the item is being used by a living being it becomes very difficult to reshape it. Some metals are very difficult to form, and may require multiple checks and a crafting check to reshape.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shape Wood
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Varies
  • Special:
  • Type: Utility

You shape wood into another form. Each mana level allows you to form a 5’x5’x5’ square of the material. If the material has already been crafted, shaped, or formed into an object, the spell becomes difficult. If the item is being used by a living being it becomes very difficult to reshape it.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shelter
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 5’
  • Target: Single Space
  • Duration: 20 minutes
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Double the duration
  • Type: Utility

The mage creates a tent-like domed shelter that is 10’ wide and 5’ tall that protects the occupants from natural weather. Each additional mana gives an additional 5’ to each side and an additional 20 minutes.

The shelter can protect against temperatures from 0 degrees to 115 degrees, as well as snow to acidic rain. It will not protect from extreme events, like a magical ice storm or a rain of fiery lava.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Shield
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Self
  • Target: Self
  • Duration: 10 minutes per mana
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles the duration
  • Type: Buff

You summon a shimmering shield that floats in front of you or is attached to your arm. It weights nothing and does not hinder spellcasting in any way. The magical shield gives you +2 AV and blocks any Mage Bolts directed at you. The shield has a total of 25 hp. Each additional mana doubles the duration and HP, but the AV is always 2. You may also charge the shield with elemental energy for 1 mana, giving bonuses based on the elemental focus you choose.

Any creature striking you with its body or a handheld weapon deals normal damage, but at the same time the attacker takes 1d6+1 points of elemental damage. Creatures wielding weapons with reach are not subject to this damage if they attack you.

The special powers of each version are as follows:

Fire Shield

The flames are warm to the touch. You take only half damage from ice-based attacks. You cast light for 30 ft. Successful attacks from water or ice-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Cold Shield

You take only half damage from fire-based attacks. Successful attacks from air-based or fire-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Water Shield

You take only half damage from fire, acid, and earth-based attacks. Successful attacks from air-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Electric Shield

You take only half damage from air and earth-based attacks. Successful attacks from water-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Earth Shield

You take only half damage from fire-based attacks. Successful attacks from air-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Air Shield

You take only half damage from air and electrical-based attacks. Successful attacks from earth-based attacks cut the remaining duration in half.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Slow
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1d4 rounds
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spirit vs. Spirit
  • Special: Save is difficult
  • Type: Debuff

The target becomes slowed. A slowed creature moves at 1/2 speed and loses half of their attacks (rounded up). A creature may attempt a save as a Move or Standard actions once per round after the first.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Spirit Sap
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 3
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 30’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spirit vs. Spirit
  • Special:  Save difficult, Critical: Save very difficult
  • Type: Debuff

You drain spirit from the target into the ether. The target loses 1d3 Spirit Points from the target for every 3 mana spent. 

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and doesn’t work.

item.title: Summon Minor Elemental
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 2
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 30’
  • Target: An empty 5’ space
  • Duration: 1 minute per mana
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Gains 1d6 HP
  • Type: Summoning

Summons a minor elemental to fight and complete tasks. A summoner can only call forth an elemental based on their elemental foci. The base elemental has 10 HP, 45% chance to hit with its fist, and does 1d4+1 damage plus the elemental damage bonus. It gains 5 HP, 10% chance to hit, and 1d4+1 damage with each additional mana level. Minor Elementals have no mind of their own and have no Dodge or Parry skill. Elemental damage of an opposing element does double damage, while elemental damage of the same element heals. The max mana that can be spent on a minor elemental is 8.

The elemental can complete simple tasks if given, but it is mindless and has no rational thought or instinct. Elemental takes double damage from any other elemental based damage.

 Element Damage: Attacks from the wraith also do elemental damage. This can be resisted with spells, magic items, or enchantments.

  • Earth: 1d6 crushing damage
  • Air: Knockback and 1d4 damage. Agility to save on knockback.
  • Water: Suffocation*. *The water creeps into the target's body, causing them to not be able to breathe for 1 round, making all tasks difficult.
  • Fire: 1d3 damage plus starts target on fire. Agility to save on fire.
  • Electricity: 1d4 electrical damage which adds +2 to damage vs characters in metal armor.
  • Ice: 1d3 cold damage plus slows the target by half. 

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles and doesn’t work.

item.title: Transform
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 15 minutes per mana
  • Avoidance: Contested Roll Spirit vs. Spirit
  • Special: Doubles the duration
  • Type: Utility

Use this spell to change a targeted being or object into a variety of shapes from the same relative type (animal to animal, vegetable to vegetable, mineral to mineral, etc.). Each level affects 3 SIZ of the target if the mage can overcome the target’s ACU in a resistance roll. If the target is willing (or the mage themself), no resistance roll is required. To target something larger than 3 SIZ, use enough levels to affect the creature or object’s full SIZ. The targeted being is then transformed into a creature or object of that exact SIZ. The new shape has the outward attributes and limitations of the shape (including natural abilities, if any), but characteristics remain unchanged. Whenever applicable, the target’s characteristics and skills are used, or the base rating in a newly acquired skill (such as Fly).

In addition to the number of SIZ points this spell can affect, additional levels can be used to affect more than one object (for example, casting one spell to affect two or more beings, provided enough SIZ is covered), or extending the duration of the spell by 15 minutes. When the spell is cast, the mage should specify how additional levels of the spell are being used.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Vision
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: 1 minute
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles Duration
  • Type: Utility

Use this spell to see what is happening in any area within 100 feet of a familiar area, or within 10 feet of an unfamiliar area. This gives full vision and hearing, as if in that area, allowing that point of view to have any mobility they possess, while within the spell’s range. 

Each additional level allows viewing of a place or events that have transpired for up to +1 additional day. When viewing an area, the caster’s perception of time is unaltered—10 combat rounds of the spell take 10 combat rounds to view—but can be ended at any time by the caster.

This spell may also be used to touch an item and receive a vision of its previous user or owner. Each additional level used gives another use or another mental image. When casting, designate how to allocate extra levels. Furthermore, each level of this spell allows a cumulative 10% chance of learning the activating word for a magical device. This spell can only be attempted once for any single object until the spell’s skill ranking is increased by at least +5%.

While using the Vision spell, the caster may not use any other spell or the Vision spell’s effects end.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Ward
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Single Target or Space
  • Duration: 2 hours per mana
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Doubles Duration
  • Type: Utility

You create a warded sphere 10’ x 10’ by touching it. Anything that moves through the warded space can do one of the following:

A loud noise, such as a wail or scream can be heard for all within 100’.

The spellcaster is alerted silently to the ward being disturbed.

The spellcaster can designate people or items that will not trigger the alarm.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Warding Armor
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: Touch
  • Target: Self
  • Duration: 1 hour
  • Avoidance: None
  • Special: Double Duration; Critical: Triple Duration
  • Type: Buff

A magical force protects your body, providing +1 AV of armor per mana spent. The maximum AV to be gained from this spell is +4. It lasts one hour. Warding Armor provides AV against mundane and magical damage, but not against elemental damage. It causes no encumbrance, but it does appear to a See Magic spell. If the caster is wearing any iron, steel, or tritium, the spell is negated.

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Web
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 2
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 60’
  • Target: 5’ spherical area
  • Duration: 1d4+1 rounds
  • Avoidance: Dodge
  • Special: Doubles the Duration Critical: Max Duration
  • Type: Trap

The mage summons thick spider-like webbing in a space. Those within the space become snared by the sticky web. The webbing needs three points of contact within the effected spell area to stay in three dimensions, otherwise it appears and falls to the ground (but may still effectively snare those in the area). Not only does the web stop or slow those within it, it makes all skill checks Difficult until the character is free of the webs.

A successful Dodge avoidance allows the character to move through the webbing at 1/2 speed. If caught in the webbing, a character can free themselves with a Difficult Effort check, which would allow them to move at half speed. 

Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.

item.title: Enfeeble
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 30’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Stamina
  • Special: Add an extra 5%; Critical Add 10%
  • Type: Debuff

The target of the spell becomes weaker and its next skill check is -5%. Each mana level adds another negative 5%.

  • Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.
  • Spellcraft Fumble: The spell targets the caster.

item.title: Sleep
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
• Mana per Level: 1
  • Casting Time: 1 Standard Action
  • Range: 30’
  • Target: Single Target
  • Duration: Instant
  • Avoidance: Spirit
  • Special: Doubles Time; Critical: Triples Time
  • Type: Mind

The target of the spell falls asleep for 1 minute. Each mana level adds another minute.

  • Spellcraft failure: The spell fizzles, and mana is lost.
  • Spellcraft Fumble: The spell targets the caster.

item.title: NPC and Creature Calculator
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Select NPC base

SagaBorn NPC & Boss Builder (Stat Block Output)

Attacks

Boss Traits (CV Modifiers)

CV: —

item.title: 11.1 SagaBorn D100 Bestiary
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
The SagaBorn d100 Creature Compendium is a complete bestiary designed for the SagaBorn d100 RPG, a system built on the foundation of Basic Roleplaying (BRP). Inspired by the classic Monster Manuals of tabletop roleplaying, this book collects creatures both familiar and unique, giving StoryGuide's and players a powerful resource for populating their campaigns with threats, allies, and mysteries.

The creatures presented here are drawn from the Dark Return setting, SagaBorn’s dark fantasy world of Uteria, where shadows linger and every monster has a story. While rooted in this setting, the monsters are designed with flexibility in mind: they can be used in any BRP or D100 campaign, from gritty survival horror to high fantasy adventures. This balance of lore-rich design and universal mechanics makes the Creature Compendium both a world guide and a practical monster manual.

Creature Compendium I

Written by Michael Bielaczyc and Dane Clark Collins. Illustrations by Michael and Paul Bielaczyc.

  • Angroll
  • Animals
  • Ank
  • Ankheg
  • Apparition
  • Ashiron
  • Babau
  • Basilisk
  • Beelzebuf
  • Beh’ul
  • Boggart
  • Bulette
  • Centipede
  • Cockatrice
  • Devouring Ooze
  • Dire Rat
  • Drake
  • Drekava
  • Dretch
  • Drogga
  • Drueglem
  • Dryad
  • Dweran
  • Dworv
  • Dwarven Drone
  • Earth Wraith
  • Elf
  • Elfling
  • Ettercap
  • Ettin
  • Faun
  • Frost Worm
  • Ghoul
  • Ghul
  • Glimmer Worm
  • Goblins
  • Clay Golem
  • Flesh Golem
  • Lesser Flesh Golem
  • Iron Golem
  • Root Golem
  • Stone Golem
  • Ghul
  • Gorgalug
  • Gnoll
  • Green Hag
  • Grelm
  • Griffon
  • Hell Hound
  • Hezrou
  • Horse
  • Hulking Horror
  • Imp
  • Kira
  • Kuduu
  • Kyzk
  • Lakava
  • Lich
  • Merfolk
  • Miglin
  • Minotaur
  • Mummy
  • Nachzur
  • Night Hag
  • Nightcat
  • Nygotha
  • Ogre
  • Orillot
  • Orlock
  • Orog
  • Orovari Warrior
  • Orvocorpse
  • Otyugh
  • Phase Cat
  • Pseudodragon
  • Pyrg
  • Ravenous Corpse
  • Reaver
  • Red Howler
  • Redcap
  • Sarap
  • Sea Hag
  • Sirin
  • Skeleton
  • Skeleton, Eldar
  • Spiders
  • Spider, Death’s Eye
  • Succubus
  • Tendril Wurm
  • Tentacle Crawler
  • Teran
  • Thundrom
  • Upir
  • Varag
  • Void Demon
  • Vorg
  • Wight
  • Wolf
  • Wyvere
  • Zombie

Character Gallery

Digital Pregen Characters: Archeon, Hunter, Luminar (wizard), Spy, Thief, Warrior, Wylder

More:

Unlike many bestiaries that focus only on monsters, this compendium provides full d100 stat blocks tailored to the SagaBorn ruleset, with clear formatting for quick reference during play. From common animals and beasts to terrifying horrors of the Dark Return setting, each entry includes lore, stats, and optional harvesting notes for players who want deeper interaction with the world.

For those familiar with Chaosium’s BRP system and other percentile-based games, the SagaBorn d100 Bestiary feels immediately accessible. Everything is percentile-driven, ensuring that rolls, attacks, defenses, and skills use the intuitive d100 format. Whether you are coming from BRP, RuneQuest, Call of Cthulhu, or any other d100 system, this compendium serves as both a monster manual and a worldbuilding guide for SagaBorn.

With dozens of original monsters, balanced stat tiers (from minions to bosses), and easy-to-use challenge values, the Creature Compendium is the perfect tool for any group seeking a BRP bestiary for d100 play. It is at once a familiar monster manual and a unique expansion into the dark fantasy world of Uteria, where every creature tells a story.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the SagaBorn d100 Creature Compendium, a Basic Roleplaying Bestiary and monster manual for BRP and D100 games.

item.title: Angroll
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Armor-plated and hunched, an angroll is a fearsome foe—and they never travel alone.

Angrolls are a large, armored race of beings who seemingly hate all other races of Uteria. They appear to be related to the common armadillo, but their own lore denies this. They are a couple heads taller than humans, but are often slumped forward, giving them the appearance of being shorter. They are a dirty race, their armored shells often coated in dirt and matted hair hanging long from their heads. Although they seem able to speak a guttural common tongue, one usually hears only a shout before they attack.
