Angrolls are cowardly creatures, never attacking unless they have superior numbers. They use all manner of weapons, usually scavenged or stolen.

Angrolls live in tribes along the Wildelands, the Wastelands, and Jaldur Desert.

—From the Bestiary Tome of Cianan

We often look down upon the filthier creatures of our world, but what is one to expect of a creature confined for so many generations to the deserts and wastes of Atheles? Might this not fuel their anger and aggression? As an experiment, I would like to take a group of them in, bathe them, and feed them well, and see if their legendary aggression might recede, and if our own arrogant impression of them as filthy creatures might fade.

—Delgon

Angroll

CV: 2

Class: Being

Type: Terian

Speed: 25

HP: 14

Damage Modifier: 1d4

Armor Value: 2

Attack: Weapon (Spear or Sword) 60% 1d8+1+1d4

Skills: Spot 50%, Survival 60% Other Skills 25%

Special: Hold Breath, Immune Disease, Create Sandstorm, Shrill Scream, Curled Defense

Weaknesses:  None

Horror: N/A

Treasure:  Standard

Special Abilities

Hold Breath 

Despite living in the desert, an angroll can hold its breath for a number of minutes equal to half its Constitution score before it risks drowning.

Immunity (Disease)

Angrolls are immune to almost all diseases and their effects. While immune, they can still carry and infect other creatures.

Create Sand Storm 

If an angroll is burrowed when first encountered, it creates a violent cloud of sand and dust, in a 20-foot radius, upon bursting from the ground. Any non-angroll creature caught in this area without eye protection must make an Agility save, or be blinded for 1d3 rounds. Whether the character succeeds the save or not, 50% concealment applies for 1d4 rounds until the sand settles.

Shrill Scream 

Once per hour, an angroll can, as a standard action, emit a loud, piercing scream. Any non-angroll creature within 30 feet of the angroll must make a Spirit save or become shaken for 1d4 rounds. Creatures that succeed at this save cannot be affected again by the same angroll's scream for 24 hours. Creatures that are already shaken become frightened for 1d4 rounds instead.

As a free action, an angroll can join in another angroll's scream. The save becomes difficult and adds +1 round to the shaken duration for each additional angroll joining in the scream.

Curled Defense

An angroll can, as a full-round action, curl into a ball and use its natural armor plating to protect itself. It becomes invulnerable to natural attacks and gains 10 AV to all other attacks. Curling into a defensive ball while in melee provokes an attack of opportunity, and the angroll is considered flat-footed while taking this action. It requires 1 round to uncurl from this position, and while uncurling, it is considered helpless.

Infected Equipment

Angrolls are filthy creatures, and their equipment is just as disgusting. If any character takes equipment or items from an angroll's body or lair without cleaning it well, they have a 50% chance to contract an illness from the items.

item.title: Ank
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
A large lizard-like creature used as a herd animal and food producer in the desolate places of the world, the ank is a staple the deserts and wastes.

Anks are large, semi-domesticated, lizard-like creatures that travel the Wildelands in herds. They are useful in the dry lands due to their unique water preservation ability. They chew grasses and plants and deposit a honey-like substance into pouches near its neck. Originally, this was a survival technique for the ank alone, but as they became domesticated, they have been bred to produce a much larger supply of the substance. Not only are anks extremely useful as pack animals in the deep desert, but are also great sources of nourishment in barren lands. 

—From the Journal of Jarin Plainswalker

Ank

CV: 2

Class: Animal

Type: Terian Animal

Speed: 25

HP: 22

Damage Modifier: 1d4

Armor Value: 2

Attack: Bite 60% 1d6+2+1d4

Skills: Spot 60%, Listen 60%

Special: Honey Globule Growth, Scent

Weaknesses: None

Horror: N/A

Treasure: None

Honey Globule Growth - Once per day, at the end of the day, the ank grows two honey globules as a byproduct of the sunlight it has absorbed. Once per day, if consumed, these grant 2 Temporary hit points (24 hours), alleviates hunger and thirst, and cures 1 horror.

Scent

Scent allows a creature to Spot other beings by their smell alone.

item.title: Animals
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded: Dire animals are larger more fierce versions of the base animals. They are one size tier larger, as well as +2 AV.

item.title: Ankheg
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
An ankheg is a burrowing creature who hunts by luring its prey into a trap it has set. The ankheg burrows in loose soil and creates a pit that traps the hunted. It then bores up from its hiding place to attack. It will attempt to grab its prey and drag it back into the tunnel as the loose soil closes up above.

The ankheg grows to approximately 10 feet’ long and weighs around 800 lbs.

—Creaturi Obscuria

Ankheg

CV: 3

Class: Monster

Type: Aberration

Speed: 25

HP: 42

Damage Modifier: 2d6

Armor Value: 4

Attack: Bite 75% 1d8+2+2d6 plus grab; Acid Spit 65% 

Skills: Dodge 65%, Other Skills 35%

Special: Spit Acid, Grab, Tremorsense 60 ft.

Weaknesses: 

Horror: 1/1d8

Treasure: Incidental

Spit Acid

Once every 6 hours, an ankheg can spit a 30-foot line of acid. Creatures struck by this acid take 4d4 points of acid damage (Agility save halves damage). Once an ankheg uses this attack, it must wait 6 hours before using it again. Additionally, during this time period, its bite attack does not inflict any additional acid damage. As a result, an ankheg does not use this ability unless it is desperate or frustrated, most often spitting acid when reduced to fewer than half its full normal hit points or when it cannot not successfully grab an opponent.

item.title: Goblins
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Goblins are a varied folk, and they have lived in Athleles for as long as there have been records. The goblin's belief is that they were formed in a dark outer realm by some of the old gods to bring chaos.

A small creature looks at you through marble-like eyes. Its skin is an unhealthy pale color of something that sees little sun, and its hair and nails are yellow. Its leather armor smells of dead animals and old dirt. It raises a wickedly notched blade menacingly. 

Goblins may be one of the most despised beings in Atheles. Elflings, known for their nonviolence and happy demeanor, turn to assault whenever they encounter goblins. Goblins are petty, vengeful, torturing little creatures who enjoy the pain of others. If there is a cause which can hurt others, goblins will flock to it like flies to dung. It is the sages of the towers who debate what made them this way, but for hunters, it is often just part of the job to clean out areas infested with the hateful things.

Many a young adventurer’s first contract is to root out the goblins from some new lair, though some of those young explorers often end up as broken skeletons in those same lairs. 

—Etahn

Goblin Commoner (Very Easy CV 1/2)

CV: 1/2

Class: Being

Type: Goblinoid

Speed: 15’

HP: 8

Damage Modifier: -2

Armor Value: 1

Attack: Short Spear 45% 1d6 -2; (Thrown) Short Spear 35% 1d6-2 (40’)

Skills: Climb 25%, Dodge 45%, Hide 45%, Spot 35%, Stealth 45%, 25% (All others)

Treasure: Average

Rough Hide, Primitive Short Spear or Rusty Short Sword, dried unknown meat, 1d6 smooth rocks, 1d4 copper coins.

Goblin Fighter (Easy CV 1)

CV: 1

Class: Being

Type: Goblinoid

Speed: 15’

HP: 13

Damage Modifier: -1

Armor Value: 1

Attack: Short Spear 50% 1d6 -1; or Short Sword 50% 1d6-1; (Thrown) Short Spear 45% 1d6-1 (40’)

Skills: Climb 25%, Dodge 45%, Hide 45%, Spot 35%, Stealth 45%, 25% (All others)

Treasure: Average

Rough Hide Armor, Primitive Short Spear or Rusty Short Sword, dried unknown meat, 1d6 smooth rocks, 1d4 copper coins.

Goblin Grabber (Average CV 2)

CV: 2

Class: Being

Type: Goblinoid

Speed: 15’

HP: 15

Damage Modifier: 0

Armor Value: 2

Attack: Hook Blade 65% 1d6+1; (Thrown) Short Spear 55% 1d6+1 (40’)

Skills: Climb 25%, Dodge 55%, Grapple 60%Hide 55%, Shield 60%, Spot 35%, Stealth 55%, Ride 35%, 25% (All others)

Treasure: Average

Rough Hide Armor, Primitive Buckler, Hooked sword, dried unknown meat, 1d2 gems, 1d4 silver coins.

Goblin grabbers are trained in evening the odds between goblins and their often taller enemies. They use their hooked swords to try and trip their oppenents. Once they are on the ground, the excle at grappling and keeping them pinned. 

Goblin Brute (Average CV 2)

CV: 2

Class: Being

Type: Goblinoid

Speed: 15’

HP: 19

Damage Modifier: 1d4

Armor Value: 2

Attack: Great Sword 65% 2d8+3+1d4; (Thrown) Short Spear 55% 1d6+1+1d4 (40’)

Skills: Climb 25%, Dodge 55%, Grapple 60%Hide 55%, Shield 60%, Spot 35%, Stealth 55%, Ride 35%, 25% (All others)

Talents: Weapon Expertise

Treasure: Average

Rough Hide Armor, Primitive Buckler, Primitive Great Sword, dried unknown meat, 1d2 gems, 1d4 gold coins.

Goblin Mystic (Average CV 2)

CV: 2

Class: Being

Type: Goblinoid

Speed: 15’

HP: 14

Damage Modifier: -1

Armor Value: 2

Attack: Dagger 45% 1d4-1; 65% Wild Magic 1d4+1

Skills: Spellcraft 70%, Climb 25%, Hide 55%, Spot 65%, Stealth 55%, Ride 35%, Dodge 55%, 25% (All others)

Talents: Spell Memory, Wild Magic

Treasure: Average

Rough hide armor, primitive buckler, silver dagger, dried unknown meat, 1d4 gems, 1d4 gold coins, 1 alchemical item.


Spells: Acid Bolt, Dispel, Heal, Protection from Arrows, Imbue Weapon

Goblin, Arnach

CV: 1

Class: Being

Type: Goblinoid

Speed: 15’

HP: 11

Damage Modifier: -1

Armor Value: 1

Attack: Short Spear 35% 1d6 -1;

Attack: Shortbow 40% 1d6

Skills: Climb 65%, Hide 45%, Spot 35%, Stealth 45%, Ride 45%, Dodge 45%, 25% (All others)

Treasure: Average

Rough Hide Armor, Rough Shortbow, Rough Short Spear, 10x arrows, 1d3 spider sac arrow, 2d6 goblin beads, unknown meat jerky, and 1d4 copper coins.

Spider Sac Arrows

Goblins can use special arrows that replace the standard arrowhead with a large spider sac. Upon a successful ranged attack, these arrows deal 1 point of damage and unleash a swarm of spiderlings upon the target. Brushing off the spiders requires a Standard Action to completely remove the spiderlings and sac. Doing so disperses the swarm.
If a character attempts to perform any action prior to wiping off the spiders, the task becomes difficult. The spider swarm deals an automatic 1 point of damage to any creature whose space it occupies at the end of their move, with no attack roll needed.

item.title: Apparition
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
A wisp of fluttering light is all that can be seen on the material plane, but in the In-Between, the apparition retains its form from its birth . . . whatever that may be.  

An apparition has been driven completely insane by its time trapped in the In-Between. I first ran into one in the farms outside of Fort Deadrick. Farmers had been found drained of life, lifeless husks littered haphazardly throughout their village during one of their local festivals. On my approach, I was attacked by a spectral creature—nothing more than a shimmering bit of mist in the fading light of day. My normal blades did no good, but a silver dagger caused it harm. It escaped our first encounter, but I tracked it to an ancient stone ring in the nearby woods. There, we battled until it fell, its deathly light stricken from our world.

—Etahn

Apparition

CV: 5
Class: Monster
Type: Navirite (Fey)
Speed: Fly 60
HP: 45
Damage Modifier: -
Armor Value: 5
Attack: Material Plane: Touch 75% 1d6 negative energy (magical damage) and Spirit Point Drain; In-Between: Claws (x2) 70% 1d8+1d4 Spirit Point Drain plus Health Drain
Skills: Acrobatics +30%, Awareness +100% (includes +50% for Heightened Senses), Dodge 60%,  Knowledge +20%, Spellcraft +20%, Survival +20%
Special: Constitution Drain, Resilient Aura, Sense the Living, Heightened Senses, Dual Plane Damage Modes
Weaknesses: Silver and Amarnium weapons can damage as if the Apparition were corporeal.
Horror: First Encounter 3/2d6+1
Treasure: None

Spirit Point Drain
 When the apparition hits with a touch attack, the target must succeed on a Stamina check. If it is successful, they suffer -1 Spirit Points, if unsuccessful, they suffer -1d6+1 Spirit Points

In-Between
If a creature is in the In-Between, the apparition uses its claws (x2) to attack instead of its touch. Each successful claw hit grants the apparition Health Drain against the target, healing it 3 HP.

Sense the Living
Apparitions are drawn to the presence of the living. In the In-Between, they can attack normally.

Heightened Senses
Apparitions have an otherworldly perception. They gain +50% to Awareness checks.Resilient Aura
Though lacking a physical form, the apparition’s supernatural aura grants it 45 HP.

item.title: Ashiron
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
She was tall, with pointed and back-turned ears. Her eyes were large, in contrast to her small features. She was taller than most men, and her figure was slim and muscled under flowing robes. She had used her wiles to convince the young duke to give her free reign over his small duchy. The ancient texts had said that an ashiron could drain a person of their life essence, maybe even their soul. From the look of the Duke, she had drained all but the most basic intelligence from him.

While my research had stated that most rare or magical metals would damage her, it was still a tough battle. She drained my strength, somehow giving it to the possessed guards, and tested my ability to know what was real and what wasn’t. But finally, my blade took her down. Although no texts mention a chance for resurrection, I still burned her body, spreading salt and silver on the ashes.

—Etahn

When an Ashiron crosses over to Atheles, they typically traverse the In-Between with their original body intact. Sometimes, if their body is destroyed, their spirit might possess another body in Atheles. If this happens, all evidence indicates that the original inhabitant of the possessed body, now trapped, with no control over his or her own body, grows increasingly mad as time passes. If the possession is not ended quickly, the original inhabitants will either remain drooling and helpless for the remainder of their lives, or will be destroyed utterly, their soul lost. A single occurrence of the Ashiron possessing an inanimate object was written of by a mage, but this could be naught but mere legend.

They often use their charisma and ability to drain people of their senses in order to gain power and status in our lands. All evidence presented shows they have little care for the inhabitants of these lands other than as vessels and food. They’re interested only in escaping the Navirim and gaining enough power to remain in our world.

—Oharad, Cryptonombus of the Navirim

Ashiron

An ashiron has the stats of any standard character or NPC. The also gain the following bonuses and abilities:

Class: Being

Type: Navirite

Speed: 25

HP: +15 to standard HP

Armor Value: +2 Natural AV

Weaknesses: Cold Iron

Magic Abilities

At will—Light; 3/day—Counter Magic 5 mana.

Spirit Warp 

As a standard Heroic Action, an ashiron can transfer an ability score, HP, or mana from a target creature and grant it to themselves, another creature, or simply destroy it. They must succeed at a contested roll of Intellect vs Spirit while the target is visible. 

The ashiron must be within 60 ft. of both targets of the transfer, and each transfer is specific to the type of transfer: 1d2 Spirit, 1d8 HP, or 1d6 mana. They can do this once per round. If the ashiron so chooses, the transferred energy can be destroyed or voided rather than transferred to a new creature.

These effects last 24 hours for those affected, or can be cured with a restoration spell or ritual.

Possess

As a standard action, an ashiron can possess the body of a medium-sized living or recently dead humanoid. A target under the effects of a Protection from the Never spell cannot be possessed.The ashiron must be in incorporeal form to attempt to possess the creature (Spirit save). Once per day, a living creature can try a Will save to expel the ashiron with a Spirit save. When an ashiron takes over a body, the consciousness of the original owner slowly falls to madness. The original owner must succeed at a Horror check each day or suffer 2/2d6 Horror. Once they lose all Horror Resistance, their spirit is lost and the body belongs to the ashiron. 

A dead creature gets no save, nor has any issue with the previous spirit of the body.

A possessed body gains all the benefits and abilities of the ashiron and loses all of its original abilities. A possessed body always has the ashiron’s statistics, and slowly morphs over a period of months to physically embody those stats. Due to their use of charisma and negotiation, ashirons tend to target beautiful individuals.

Incorporeal Form

An ashiron can leave the host body it possesses at any time. The only way to trap an ashiron in a body is with a circle of salt, or with a rune circle. This form looks like a green mist. An ashiron only takes damage from cold iron (or enchanted weapons vs Navirite fey) in incorporeal form.

item.title: Babau
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Babau’s are often used as soldiers by more powerful entities in the Navirim. They are the perfect soldiers: tough, immune to most damage, capable of multiple powerful attacks. During the Great War, the Uthgard Kingdom had a legion of babau, controlled by a powerful Dreadlord mage.

When a babau finds itself alone in our world, they often stalk a victim, relishing in its fear and distress. This has lead to a folk tale view of them as boogeymen, hiding under beds or in closets to torment their victims. In truth, if a babau stalks a person, that person will not likely last long.

—Oharad, Cryptonombus of the Navirim

Babau

CV: 4

Class: Monster

Type: Navirite

Speed: 25

HP: 35

Damage Modifier: 1d6

Armor Value: 6

Attack: (2) claws 80% 1d6+4+1d6

Skills: Dodge 75%, Hide 65%, Stealth 65%,  Other Skills 45%

Special: Darkvision, 

Weaknesses: Cold Iron, Protective Slime, Immune to acid, cold, and fire.

Horror: 2/1d8+1

Treasure: None

Protective Slime

A layer of acidic slime coats a babau’s skin. Any creature that strikes a babau with a natural attack or unarmed strike takes 1d8 points of acid damage from this slime if it fails an Agility save. A creature that strikes a babau with a melee weapon must make an Agility save, or the weapon takes 1d8 points of acid damage. Ammunition that strikes a babau is automatically destroyed after it inflicts its damage.

Sneak Attack

The babau does an extra 2d6 points of damage whenever a foe is unaware of them, flanked, or prone.

Magic Abilities

Constant–see invisibility - can see any invisible objects.At will– dark (mana 3)

item.title: Basilisk
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
A basilisk is a giant reptile that can turn flesh and blood creature to stone. They can grow up to 12 feet long from snout to tip of tail, though they have short legs and, other than their stony gaze, can only bite their prey.

It is thought that they were created by ancient wizard magic for guarding of places of power.

—Wolfram Buhl, Buc de Megichen Teir, (translated to the common tongue in 5763)

Basilisk

CV: 4

Class: Monster

Type: Magical Beast

Speed: 30’

HP: 37

Damage Modifier: 1d6

Armor Value: 5

Attack: Bite* 75% 1d6+4+1d6

Skills: Dodge 50%, Jump 40%, Climb 40%, Spot 40%, Listen 40%, Hide 24%, Stealth 24% 

Special: Stone Breath

Weaknesses: Mirrors

Horror: (If Turning to Stone) 2/2d4

Treasure: Standard (Lair)

*Bite The large mouth of a basilisk can only be dodged or parried with a shield; weapons are too small to block it.

Horror Anyone who begins to turn to stone suffers Horror at the unnatural transformation.

Stone Breath Contrary to belief, it is not the gaze of a basilisk that turns people to stone; it is its breath. Once every two rounds, as a Move or Standard action, a basilisk can breathe a 15’ cone of breath that can turn the skin of living creatures and plants into stone. A difficult Stamina check can resist the monster's breath. If it is not resisted, the skin of the affected creature or plant begins to calcify, turning to a gray stone-like surface. In the first 2 rounds, the affected creature can only move at half speed. In the next two rounds, they can no longer walk, but they can complete other actions, but any action is difficult. In the following two rounds, they can speak but nothing more. They become encased in the calci-stone, alive but unable to move. 

A creature petrified in this matter that is then coated (not just splashed) with fresh basilisk saliva (taken from a basilisk no more than 1 hour dead) is instantly restored to flesh. A single basilisk contains enough saliva to coat 1d3 Medium creatures in this manner.

Treasure A basilisk enjoys hoarding belongings from those it hunts. It likes shiny objects piling them in the back of its lair.

Average worth 600 gp. 50% coins, 30% gems and jewelry, 20% objects and equipment.

Reagents and Remnants

A basilisk skin makes a hardy cloak, able to withstand most elements and gains a +20% to rolls vs acid. They have 3 doses of saliva that can dissolve stone (a 6’x6’x1’ section reach). And they have a gland that contains a liquid that acts the same as their Stone Breath. Removing this gland is delicate, and any mishap on a Survival check means the harvester becomes contaminated with the glands contents and begins to turn.

item.title: Beelzebuf
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Imagine a mouth large enough to swallow a human whole. Attach a couple of beady eyes and toss it in the muck, and there’s your beelzebuf. Luckily they are rare, though we hear more and more reports about them coming out of the murkier places of the southeast. 

They resemble an angry, giant, mutated toad and they are always hungry. Even with their large size, they are fast, very fast. A long spear or pike is a good starting weapon. If it swallows you, well... better hope you can cut your way out with whatever blade you have on you!

—Etahn

Beelzebuf

CV: 3

Class: Animal

Type: Amphibian

Speed: 30

HP: 44

Damage Modifier: 2d6

Armor Value: 4

Attack: Bite 75% 1d6+3+2d6; Tongue 75% grab; or Venom Spit 75% (Blindness)

Skills: Dodge 65%, Hide 65%, Stealth 50%, Other Skills 35%

Special: Blinding Venom, Tongue, Grab, Swallow Whole

Weaknesses: None

Horror:

Treasure: Incidental

Tongue

A beelzebuf's tongue is an attack with reach equal to 40 feet. The tongue itself deals no damage on a hit but can be used to grab. 

Grab

When hit with an attack that also does grab, the attacker and defender must roll a Heroic Action. If the defender loses, they become grappled. They must succeed at a Heroic Action during their turn to free themselves.

Blinding Venom

A beelzebuf can spit a glob of venom up to 40 feet. This sticky liquid covers the target and can cause blindness for 2d4 rounds. The target can negate the blindness with a difficult Stamina save.

Swallow Whole

If a beelzebuf has grappled an opponent with its tongue, it can then swallow the opponent as a free action (if Medium or smaller in size) at the beginning of its turn. It can only have one victim swallowed at a time. Once swallowed, the victim takes 1d6 acid damage per round. The victim can try to climb out with a Heroic Action or damage the beelzebuf from the inside, usually by cutting one's way out (the beelzebuf has the same HP, but only half its AV). The constricted nature of being inside a beelzebuf restricts the victim to the use of one-handed weapons only. 

Reagents and Remnants

Beelzebuf Tongue

Their tongues are used as reagents in some dark rituals and for creating snare arrows.

 Value: 200 gp
Harvesting Difficulty: Difficult

Beelzebuf Venom Gland

Their venom gland can be milked to produce up to 5 vials of blinding poison.

Value: 80 gp each

Havesting Difficulty: Average

item.title: Void Demon
item.dc:creator: zerosrd
item.content:encoded:
Other names: ghaddar, nija, oz’goth

A species of demons who come from the void. They were the servants of the old ones. They hate the light and all the creatures in it. They want to destroy all worlds and bring back the void. 

They are misery and pain. They thrive on hurting. They are alien and lack any empathy. 

Their mystics have three eyes and can control others. Everyone can read surface thoughts. The leader mystics torture and body maim any others born with three eyes to stay in power. When they grow old and feeble, a new leader arises. 

They breed by putting larvae in the brains of living creatures; then tiny beings squirm into a shaman’s belly pouch. There, they grow until large enough to be outside. 

Most oz'goth are little more than followers and footsoldiers. They thrive on hate and violence, extinguishing any light or life they come across. 

They are living creatures but do not have internal organs—just a strange black goo with various gelatinous spheres. This internal fluid is very poisonous to most living creatures. Whether it is ingested or touches the skin, it causes a sickness that is hard to cure.

While they have no need for eating, they crave eating the spoils of their destruction. All treasure an oz’goth has been swallowed and resides inside their grotesque form.

They have no language; instead, they speak through mental emotions and images. 

Oz’goth Grunt

  • CV: 2
  • Class: Monster
  • Type: Navirite
  • Speed: 25
  • HP: 16
  • Damage Modifier: 0
  • Armor Value: 2 + Prickly Skin
  • Attack: (2) Claw 1d6+2+1d4 Wither
  • Skills: Dodge 50%, Grapple 50%, Hide 50%, Listen 50%, Spot 50%, Stealth 50%, Other Skills 25%
  • Special: Darkvision 60 ft.; Immunity to Blindness, Poison
  • Weaknesses: Cold Iron (double damage), Light, Fear
  • Horror: First encounter 2/2d3
  • Treasure: Half Normal (ingested)

Light Weakness

Anything brighter than torchlight causes the oz’goth pain and partial blindness. They take a bane (1d6) to all rolls when in bright light and 1 damage per round.

Poisonous Blood

Oz’goth Blood - contact or ingestion; POT 7; intense pain, weakness

The black viscous fluid that fills an Oz’goth is poisonous to most living creatures. When it touches the skin or is ingested, it immediately causes pain and damage. Afterward, the creature becomes sick over the next day. They slowly lose health at 1 HP per hour. A successful Stamina check negates the sickness.

Prickly Skin

Needle-like spikes poke out of the purplish flesh of the oz’goth. The spikes protrude and retract as if something alive under the skin. When anyone hits a oz’goth with a melee attack, they must succeed at an Agility roll or take 1d4 damage.

Insight

As a standard or move action, an oz’goth can attempt a Heroic Action to read the surface thoughts of its target. If it succeeds, it gains a Boon to its next roll against that target. This bonus only lasts 1 round.

Rend

If an oz’goth hits with both claw attacks, it latches onto the opponent’s body and tears the flesh. This attack automatically deals an additional 2d6 points of damage.

Fear

1/day— fear (like spell, mana 1)

Oz’goth Mystic

  • CV: 3
  • Class: Monster
  • Type: Navirite
  • Speed: 25
  • HP: 24
  • Damage Modifier: 0
  • Armor Value: 2
  • Attack: (2) Claw 75% 1d6+1+1d4 Wither; Ranged: Void Bolt 75% 1d6+1+1d4 Wither
  • Skills: Spellcraft 65%, Dodge 50%, Grapple 50%, Hide 50%, Listen 50%, Spot 50%, Stealth 50%, Other Skills 25%
  • Special: Darkvision 60 ft.; Immunity to Blindness, Poison, Fear
  • Weaknesses: Cold Iron (double damage), Light
  • Horror: First encounter 2/2d3
  • Treasure: Normal (ingested)

Light Weakness

Anything brighter than torchlight causes the oz’goth pain and partial blindness. They take a bane (1d6) to all rolls when in bright light and 1 damage per round.

Poisonous Blood

Oz’goth Blood - injury or ingestion; POT 7; intense pain, weakness

The black viscous fluid that fills an Oz’goth is poisonous to most living creatures. When it touches the skin or is ingested, it immediately causes pain and damage. Afterward, the creature becomes sick over the next day. They slowly lose health at 1 HP per hour. A successful Stamina check negates the poison.

Prickly Skin

Needle-like spikes poke out of the purplish flesh of the oz’goth. The spikes protrude and retract as if something alive under the skin. When anyone hits a oz’goth with a melee attack, they must succeed at an Agility roll or take 1d4 damage.

Insight

As a standard or move action, an oz’goth can attempt a Heroic Action to read the surface thoughts of its target. If it succeeds, it gains a Boon to its next roll against that target. This bonus only lasts 1 round.

Rend

If an oz’goth hits with both claw attacks, it latches onto the opponent’s body and tears the flesh. This attack automatically deals an additional 2d6 points of damage.

Fear

1/day— fear (like spell, mana 1)

Magic:

Mana: 15

Spells: Befriend, Counter Magic, Dark, Dispel, Spirit Sap
